---
title: NY HP Rate Design Analysis
reference-location: margin
fig-cap-location: bottom
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This notebook contains the analysis behind the NY Heat Pump Rates report.
Starting from CAIRO simulation outputs on S3, it computes every figure, table,
and inline statistic that appears in the report narrative (`index.qmd`).

The analysis follows the report's arc:

1. **Bill components** — how a representative home's energy bill is structured,
   and how it changes after switching to a heat pump.
2. **Bill change impact** — statewide distributions of bill changes under
   default rates, sliced by heating fuel type and LMI status.
3. **Cross-subsidization** — the Bill Alignment Test (BAT), showing that heat
   pump customers systematically overpay for delivery under current rates.
4. **Fair rate proposal** — a seasonal delivery rate that eliminates the
   cross-subsidy, and its impact on HP and non-HP customers.
5. **Energy burden** — how switching to heat pumps affects low-income
   households' energy burden under different LMI discount scenarios.

Diagnostic histograms (bill change and BAT distributions by fuel type and
utility) are in the companion `data_quality.qmd` notebook.

## Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
import math

import polars as pl
from great_tables import GT
from matplotlib.figure import Figure
from lib.rdp import fetch_rdp_file
from lib.plotnine import SB_COLORS, theme_switchbox
from lib.quarto import display_svg
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    annotate,
    coord_cartesian,
    coord_flip,
    element_blank,
    element_text,
    facet_grid,
    facet_wrap,
    geom_col,
    geom_hline,
    geom_segment,
    geom_step,
    geom_text,
    geom_vline,
    ggplot,
    guides,
    labs,
    position_stack,
    scale_color_identity,
    scale_fill_gradient,
    scale_fill_manual,
    scale_x_continuous,
    scale_x_discrete,
    scale_y_continuous,
    theme,
)

report_vars: dict = {}


def _weighted_percentile(df: pl.DataFrame, col: str, w_col: str, q: float) -> float:
    """Weighted percentile via cumulative weight."""
    s = df.sort(col)
    cum = s[w_col].cum_sum() / s[w_col].sum()
    return float(s.filter(cum >= q)[col][0])


def _weighted_mean(df: pl.DataFrame, col: str, w_col: str) -> float:
    """Weighted arithmetic mean."""
    return float((df[col] * df[w_col]).sum() / df[w_col].sum())
```

## Parameters

```{python}
#| label: params

# --- To switch states, change these values and the 4 utility dicts in the constants cell below. ---
INCLUDE_TOU = True  # set False to skip TOU rate cells (e.g. when TOU run is not available)
UTILITY = "coned"  # any utility code from UTIL_NAMES, used for the representative-building example
STATE = "NY"
YEAR = 2025

### UPDATE WITH NEW RUNS
BATCH = "ny_20260408_all_epmc_r1-24"
RDP_REF = "530564d"  # rate-design-platform git ref for fetch_rdp_file
###


### UPDATE TO SWITCH RESIDUAL ALLOCATION METHODS
# Delivery residual allocation: sets BAT column stubs, run pairs, and tariff filename slugs together.
RESIDUAL_ALLOC_DELIVERY = "epmc"  # "percustomer" | "epmc"

if RESIDUAL_ALLOC_DELIVERY == "percustomer":
    # HP seasonal precalc (u0), HP seasonal default (u2), TOU flex default (u2)
    RUN_HPRATE = "5+6"
    RUN_HPRATE_HP = "7+8"
    RUN_TOU_HP = "15+16"
    TARIFF_SEASONAL = "hp_seasonal"
    TARIFF_TOU = "hp_seasonalTOU_flex"
elif RESIDUAL_ALLOC_DELIVERY == "epmc":
    RUN_HPRATE = "21+22"
    RUN_HPRATE_HP = "23+24"
    RUN_TOU_HP = "27+28"
    TARIFF_SEASONAL = "hp_seasonal_epmc"
    TARIFF_TOU = "hp_seasonalTOU_flex_epmc"
else:
    msg = f"Unknown RESIDUAL_ALLOC_DELIVERY {RESIDUAL_ALLOC_DELIVERY!r}; use 'percustomer' or 'epmc'"
    raise ValueError(msg)

RESIDUAL_ALLOC_SUPPLY = "percustomer"  # "percustomer" | "passthrough" — supply BAT / residual column stubs only
###


# Everything below derives from the params above
_state_lower = STATE.lower()
S3_BASE = "s3://data.sb/switchbox/cairo/outputs/hp_rates"
_BILLS = f"{S3_BASE}/{_state_lower}/all_utilities/{BATCH}"
_BAT = _BILLS

PATH_MASTER_BILLS_BASELINE = f"{_BILLS}/run_1+2/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_DEFAULT_HP = f"{_BILLS}/run_3+4/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_HPRATE = f"{_BILLS}/run_{RUN_HPRATE}/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_HPRATE_HP = f"{_BILLS}/run_{RUN_HPRATE_HP}/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_TOU_HP = f"{_BILLS}/run_{RUN_TOU_HP}/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BAT_BASELINE = f"{_BAT}/run_1+2/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"
PATH_MASTER_BAT_DEFAULT_HP = f"{_BAT}/run_3+4/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"
PATH_MASTER_BAT_HPRATE = f"{_BAT}/run_{RUN_HPRATE}/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"

PATH_RESSTOCK = "s3://data.sb/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2"
```

## Constants

```{python}
#| label: constants

ANNUAL_MONTH = "Annual"
BLDG_ID = "bldg_id"
HEATING_TYPE_COL = "postprocess_group.heating_type"
INCOME_COL = "in.representative_income"
BURDEN_THRESHOLD = 0.06
LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT = 40
report_vars["lmi_current_enrollment_pct"] = LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT
LMI_BILL_COLS = ["elec_total_bill", "gas_total_bill", "oil_total_bill", "propane_total_bill"]
LMI_40_BILL_COLS = ["elec_total_bill_lmi_40", "gas_total_bill_lmi_40", "oil_total_bill", "propane_total_bill"]
LMI_100_BILL_COLS = ["elec_total_bill_lmi_100", "gas_total_bill_lmi_100", "oil_total_bill", "propane_total_bill"]
UTIL_NAMES = {  # state-dependent: swap when switching states
    "cenhud": "Central Hudson",
    "coned": "Con Edison",
    "nimo": "National Grid",
    "nyseg": "NYSEG",
    "or": "O&R",
    "psegli": "PSEG-LI",
    "rge": "RG&E",
}
UTIL_ORDER_CODES = ["psegli", "coned", "or", "cenhud", "nyseg", "rge", "nimo"]
UTIL_DISPLAY: dict[str, str] = {  # short names for figures / by-utility tables
    "coned": "ConEd",
    "nimo": "NiMo",
    "nyseg": "NYSEG",
    "cenhud": "CenHud",
    "rge": "RG&E",
    "or": "O&R",
    "psegli": "PSEG-LI",
}
TARIFF_UTILS: dict[str, str] = {  # display name -> tariff code for rate_design/hp_rates/{state}/config/tariffs/electric
    "ConEd": "coned",
    "NiMo": "nimo",
    "NYSEG": "nyseg",
    "CenHud": "cenhud",
    "RG&E": "rge",
    "O&R": "or",
    "PSEG-LI": "psegli",
}
UTIL_SHORT: dict[str, str] = {  # tariff code -> short label for BAT-by-utility table columns
    "cenhud": "CenHud",
    "coned": "ConEd",
    "nimo": "NiMo",
    "nyseg": "NYSEG",
    "or": "O&R",
    "psegli": "PSEG-LI",
    "rge": "RG&E",
}

BAT_DELIVERY = f"BAT_{RESIDUAL_ALLOC_DELIVERY}_delivery"
BAT_SUPPLY = f"BAT_{RESIDUAL_ALLOC_SUPPLY}_supply"
BAT_TOTAL = "bat_total"  # derived as BAT_DELIVERY + BAT_SUPPLY in each BAT ingest cell
_res_infix = f"_{RESIDUAL_ALLOC_DELIVERY}" if RESIDUAL_ALLOC_DELIVERY != "percustomer" else ""
RESIDUAL_DELIVERY = f"residual_share{_res_infix}_delivery"
```

## Data ingest

The master bills tables are pre-built, Hive-partitioned parquet datasets that contain per-building monthly and annual
bills with fully decomposed electric components (fixed, delivery volumetric, supply), gas, oil, and propane — plus
ResStock metadata columns — across all NY utilities.

```{python}
#| label: ingest

bills_current = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_BASELINE, hive_partitioning=True)
bills_hp = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_DEFAULT_HP, hive_partitioning=True)
bills_hprate_current = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_HPRATE, hive_partitioning=True)
bills_hprate_hp = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_HPRATE_HP, hive_partitioning=True)
bills_tou_hp = (
    pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_TOU_HP, hive_partitioning=True)
    if INCLUDE_TOU
    else None
)
```

## Bill components: how energy bills change after switching

This section builds the stacked bar charts that show how a representative
gas-heated home's energy bill breaks down — and how each component changes
after switching to a heat pump. We track a single building (the statewide
weighted-median natgas home with full baseline cooling) so the reader can see
gas costs shrink and electric costs grow for one household.

```{python}
#| label: bill-component-helpers

def bill_components_for_scenario(
    bills_lf: pl.LazyFrame,
    utility: str | None = None,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Extract annual bill components (electric + gas + delivered fuels) from master bills."""
    filtered = bills_lf.filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    if utility is not None:
        filtered = filtered.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == utility)
    return (
        filtered
        .select(
            BLDG_ID, "weight",
            pl.col("elec_supply_bill").alias("supply"),
            pl.col("elec_delivery_bill").alias("delivery_volumetric"),
            pl.col("elec_fixed_charge").alias("delivery_fixed"),
            pl.col("gas_total_bill").alias("gas"),
            pl.col("propane_total_bill").alias("propane"),
            pl.col("oil_total_bill").alias("oil"),
            pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("energy_total"),
            "heats_with_natgas",
            "in.hvac_cooling_partial_space_conditioning",
        )
        .collect()
    )


def weighted_median_row(
    df: pl.DataFrame,
    sort_col: str = "energy_total",
    weight_col: str = "weight",
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Return the single row at the weighted median by sort_col."""
    total_weight = df[weight_col].sum()
    sorted_df = df.sort(sort_col)
    cum = sorted_df[weight_col].cum_sum()
    idx = (cum >= 0.5 * total_weight).arg_max()  # first True in a bool Series = first row past 50%
    assert idx is not None, "Empty data or non-positive weights"
    return sorted_df.slice(idx, 1)


SELECT_COLS = [
    BLDG_ID, "weight",
    "supply", "delivery_volumetric", "delivery_fixed", "gas",
    "propane", "oil", "energy_total",
]
```

```{python}
#| label: bill-component-plot-helpers

SCENARIO_ORDER = ["Natural gas furnace", "Air-source heat pump"]

COMPONENT_NAMES: dict[str, str] = {
    "delivery_fixed": "Electric Delivery Bill (Fixed)",
    "delivery_volumetric": "Electric Delivery Bill (Volumetric)",
    "supply": "Electric Supply Bill",
    "gas": "Gas Bill",
}
COMPONENT_ORDER = list(COMPONENT_NAMES.values())
DECOMPOSED_COLORS = {
    "Electric Delivery Bill (Fixed)": "#023047",
    "Electric Delivery Bill (Volumetric)": "#56B4E9",
    "Electric Supply Bill": "#E69F00",
    "Gas Bill": "#CCCCCC",
}
DECOMPOSED_LABEL_COLORS = {**DECOMPOSED_COLORS, "Gas Bill": "#888888"}

LINE_X = 2.35
LABEL_X = 2.45


def _format_dollar(v: float) -> str:
    return f"${v:,.0f}"


def _dollar_labels(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    return df.with_columns(
        pl.col("value")
        .map_elements(_format_dollar, return_dtype=pl.String)
        .alias("label"),
    )


def _totals_df(plot_df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    return _dollar_labels(
        plot_df.group_by("scenario").agg(pl.col("value").sum())
    )


def _side_label_annotations(
    segments: list[tuple[str, float, float]],
    label_colors: dict[str, str],
) -> list:
    """Build annotate() layers for side-labels pointing at bar 2 segments.

    *segments* is a list of ``(label_text, bottom_y, height)`` tuples.
    """
    annotations: list = []
    for label_text, bottom, height in segments:
        y_mid = bottom + height / 2
        color_key = label_text.replace("\n", " ")
        color = label_colors[color_key]
        annotations.append(annotate(
            "segment",
            x=LINE_X, xend=LABEL_X - 0.03,
            y=y_mid, yend=y_mid,
            color=color, size=0.5,
        ))
        annotations.append(annotate(
            "text",
            x=LABEL_X, y=y_mid,
            label=label_text,
            color=color, ha="left", va="center",
            size=8, fontweight="bold",
        ))
    return annotations


# ── Decomposed chart (electric sub-components + muted gas + delivery line) ───

def unpivot_median(median_df: pl.DataFrame, scenario: str) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Unpivot a single-row median into long-form (scenario, component, value)."""
    cols = list(COMPONENT_NAMES.keys())
    return (
        median_df.select(cols)
        .unpivot(on=cols, variable_name="col", value_name="value")
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("col").replace_strict(COMPONENT_NAMES).alias("component"),
            pl.lit(scenario).alias("scenario"),
        )
        .drop("col")
    )


def plot_bill_components_decomposed(
    med_current: pl.DataFrame,
    med_hp: pl.DataFrame,
    title: str = "Median energy bill components",
) -> ggplot:
    """Stacked bar with electric decomposition, muted gas, and delivery reference line."""
    long = pl.concat([
        unpivot_median(med_current, SCENARIO_ORDER[0]),
        unpivot_median(med_hp, SCENARIO_ORDER[1]),
    ])

    scenario_dtype = pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)
    component_dtype = pl.Enum(COMPONENT_ORDER)

    plot_df = _dollar_labels(long.with_columns(
        pl.col("scenario").cast(scenario_dtype),
        pl.col("component").cast(component_dtype),
    ))
    totals = _totals_df(plot_df)

    delivery_top_left = float(
        med_current["delivery_fixed"][0] + med_current["delivery_volumetric"][0]
    )
    supply_top_left = delivery_top_left + float(med_current["supply"][0])

    fixed_hp = float(med_hp["delivery_fixed"][0])
    vol_hp = float(med_hp["delivery_volumetric"][0])
    supply_hp = float(med_hp["supply"][0])
    gas_hp = float(med_hp["gas"][0])

    delivery_top_right = fixed_hp + vol_hp
    supply_top_right = delivery_top_right + supply_hp

    delivery_mult = delivery_top_right / delivery_top_left
    supply_mult = supply_top_right / supply_top_left

    side_labels = _side_label_annotations(
        [
            ("Electric Delivery\nBill (Fixed)", 0, fixed_hp),
            ("Electric Delivery\nBill (Volumetric)", fixed_hp, vol_hp),
            ("Electric Supply Bill", fixed_hp + vol_hp, supply_hp),
            ("Gas Bill", fixed_hp + vol_hp + supply_hp, gas_hp),
        ],
        DECOMPOSED_LABEL_COLORS,
    )

    p = (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="scenario", y="value", fill="component"))
        + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.6)
        + geom_text(
            aes(label="label"),
            position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
            color="white", fontweight="bold", size=11,
        )
        + geom_text(
            aes(x="scenario", y="value", label="label"),
            data=totals,
            va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold",
            size=11, nudge_y=30, inherit_aes=False,
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=DECOMPOSED_COLORS)
        + scale_x_discrete(limits=SCENARIO_ORDER, expand=(0, 0.6, 0, 0.9))
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.08, 0))
        + guides(fill=False)
        + labs(x="", y="Annual energy bill ($)", title=title)
        + theme_switchbox()
    )
    for sl in side_labels:
        p = p + sl
    return p
```

### Representative home

We hardcode a specific building chosen for its narrative qualities: near-median
baseline bill, meaningful bill increase after switching, and clear delivery
underpayment before switching. See the selection criteria in the agent transcript.

```{python}
#| label: compute-bill-components-state

natgas_current_state = (
    bill_components_for_scenario(bills_current)
    .filter(                                                                     # <1>
        pl.col("heats_with_natgas")                                              # <1>
        & (pl.col("propane") == 0)                                               # <1>
        & (pl.col("oil") == 0)                                                   # <1>
    )                                                                            # <1>
)
assert not natgas_current_state.is_empty(), "No natgas-only buildings statewide (current)"

hp_same_bldgs_state = (
    bill_components_for_scenario(bills_hp)
    .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(natgas_current_state[BLDG_ID].to_list()))     # <2>
)
assert not hp_same_bldgs_state.is_empty(), "No matching buildings statewide (HP)"

median_bldg_id_state = 497234                                                    # <3>
median_current_state = (                                                         # <3>
    natgas_current_state                                                         # <3>
    .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID) == median_bldg_id_state)                            # <3>
    .select(SELECT_COLS)                                                         # <3>
)                                                                                # <3>
assert len(median_current_state) == 1, f"Hardcoded bldg {median_bldg_id_state} not found in natgas pool"
median_hp_state = (
    hp_same_bldgs_state
    .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID) == median_bldg_id_state)
    .select(SELECT_COLS)
)
assert len(median_hp_state) == 1, f"Expected 1 row for bldg {median_bldg_id_state} in HP, got {len(median_hp_state)}"

median_hp_hprate_state = (
    bill_components_for_scenario(bills_hprate_hp)
    .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID) == median_bldg_id_state)
    .select(SELECT_COLS)
)
assert len(median_hp_hprate_state) == 1, f"Expected 1 row for bldg {median_bldg_id_state} in HP hprate, got {len(median_hp_hprate_state)}"

_elec_cols = ["supply", "delivery_volumetric", "delivery_fixed"]
_elec_before = sum(median_current_state[c][0] for c in _elec_cols)
_elec_after = sum(median_hp_state[c][0] for c in _elec_cols)
_gas_before = median_current_state["gas"][0]
_gas_after = median_hp_state["gas"][0]

report_vars["median_energy_total"] = median_current_state["energy_total"][0]
report_vars["median_elec_bill_hp"] = _elec_after
report_vars["median_gas_drop"] = _gas_before - _gas_after
report_vars["median_elec_jump"] = _elec_after - _elec_before
report_vars["median_net_increase"] = report_vars["median_elec_jump"] - report_vars["median_gas_drop"]
```

1. Same natgas-only filter as the representative-building cell. Statewide, ~2.7% of natgas buildings are dropped.
2. Pre-filter the HP scenario to the same natgas-only building IDs so we can look up individual buildings.
3. Hardcoded building: 1,698 sqft, 1990s, 3-bed, 2-story SFD in Erie County (NYSEG). Baseline bill near
   the statewide median for natgas-only homes, bill increases by ~$800 after HP switch, and underpays
   ~$216/yr on per-customer delivery BAT before switching.

We also load monthly load curves for this building from ResStock to show energy consumption (in kWh) alongside the bill
charts.

```{python}
#| label: load-representative-bldg-curves

MONTH_LABELS = ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"]
LOAD_COLS = [
    "month",
    "out.electricity.total.energy_consumption",
    "out.natural_gas.total.energy_consumption",
]

load_base = str(Path("/ebs/data/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2_sb/load_curve_monthly") / f"state={STATE}")
df_before_load = pl.read_parquet(f"{load_base}/upgrade=00/{median_bldg_id_state}-0.parquet", columns=LOAD_COLS)
df_after_load = pl.read_parquet(f"{load_base}/upgrade=02/{median_bldg_id_state}-2.parquet", columns=LOAD_COLS)

elec_kwh_before = df_before_load["out.electricity.total.energy_consumption"].sum()
gas_kwh_before = df_before_load["out.natural_gas.total.energy_consumption"].sum()
elec_kwh_after = df_after_load["out.electricity.total.energy_consumption"].sum()
gas_kwh_after = df_after_load["out.natural_gas.total.energy_consumption"].sum()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-consumption-vs-bills-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual energy consumption and energy bills before and after switching to a heat pump, for the gas-heated home at the statewide median energy bill (among gas-heated homes with full baseline cooling)."

norm_kwh = elec_kwh_before + gas_kwh_before
norm_bill = _elec_before + _gas_before

report_vars["elec_share_of_energy_use"] = elec_kwh_before / norm_kwh
report_vars["elec_share_of_energy_bill"] = _elec_before / norm_bill

PANEL_ORDER = ["Annual energy use", "Annual energy bill"]
COMP_ORDER = ["Electricity", "Natural Gas"]

_rows = []
for scenario, elec_k, gas_k, elec_b, gas_b in [
    (SCENARIO_ORDER[0], elec_kwh_before, gas_kwh_before, _elec_before, _gas_before),
    (SCENARIO_ORDER[1], elec_kwh_after, gas_kwh_after, _elec_after, _gas_after),
]:
    _rows.append({"panel": "Annual energy use", "scenario": scenario, "component": "Electricity",
                  "norm_value": elec_k / norm_kwh, "label": f"{elec_k:,.0f} kWh"})
    _rows.append({"panel": "Annual energy use", "scenario": scenario, "component": "Natural Gas",
                  "norm_value": gas_k / norm_kwh, "label": f"{gas_k:,.0f} kWh"})
    _rows.append({"panel": "Annual energy bill", "scenario": scenario, "component": "Electricity",
                  "norm_value": elec_b / norm_bill, "label": f"${elec_b:,.0f}"})
    _rows.append({"panel": "Annual energy bill", "scenario": scenario, "component": "Natural Gas",
                  "norm_value": gas_b / norm_bill, "label": f"${gas_b:,.0f}"})

combined_df = pl.DataFrame(_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("panel").cast(pl.Enum(PANEL_ORDER)),
    pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)),
    pl.col("component").cast(pl.Enum(COMP_ORDER)),
)

combined_totals_rows = []
for panel, elec, gas, norm, fmt in [
    ("Annual energy use", elec_kwh_before, gas_kwh_before, norm_kwh, lambda v: f"{v:,.0f} kWh"),
    ("Annual energy use", elec_kwh_after, gas_kwh_after, norm_kwh, lambda v: f"{v:,.0f} kWh"),
    ("Annual energy bill", _elec_before, _gas_before, norm_bill, lambda v: f"${v:,.0f}"),
    ("Annual energy bill", _elec_after, _gas_after, norm_bill, lambda v: f"${v:,.0f}"),
]:
    i = len(combined_totals_rows)
    s = SCENARIO_ORDER[0] if i % 2 == 0 else SCENARIO_ORDER[1]
    combined_totals_rows.append({
        "panel": panel, "scenario": s,
        "norm_value": (elec + gas) / norm,
        "label": fmt(elec + gas),
    })

combined_totals = pl.DataFrame(combined_totals_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("panel").cast(pl.Enum(PANEL_ORDER)),
    pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)),
)

combined_labels = combined_df.filter(pl.col("norm_value") >= 0.08)

COMP_COLORS = {"Electricity": "#FFC729", "Natural Gas": "#C8D66A"}
COMP_LABEL_COLORS = {"Electricity": "#FFC729", "Natural Gas": "#7A8A10"}

elec_norm_hp_bill = _elec_after / norm_bill
gas_norm_hp_bill = _gas_after / norm_bill
side_label_rows = []
for comp, bottom, height in [
    ("Electricity", 0, elec_norm_hp_bill),
    ("Natural Gas", elec_norm_hp_bill, gas_norm_hp_bill),
]:
    side_label_rows.append({
        "panel": "Annual energy bill", "y": bottom + height / 2,
        "label_text": comp, "color": COMP_LABEL_COLORS[comp],
    })
side_labels_df = pl.DataFrame(side_label_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("panel").cast(pl.Enum(PANEL_ORDER)),
)

SIDE_LINE_X = 2.33
SIDE_LABEL_X = 2.40

p_combined = (
    ggplot(combined_df, aes(x="scenario", y="norm_value", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.6)
    + geom_text(
        combined_labels,
        aes(label="label"),
        position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
        color="white", fontweight="bold", size=10,
    )
    + geom_text(
        combined_totals,
        aes(x="scenario", y="norm_value", label="label"),
        va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold",
        size=10, nudge_y=0.02, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_segment(
        side_labels_df,
        aes(x=SIDE_LINE_X, xend=SIDE_LABEL_X - 0.02, y="y", yend="y", color="color"),
        size=0.5, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_text(
        side_labels_df,
        aes(x=SIDE_LABEL_X, y="y", label="label_text", color="color"),
        ha="left", va="center", size=8, fontweight="bold", inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + scale_color_identity()
    + facet_wrap("panel", ncol=2)
    + scale_fill_manual(values=COMP_COLORS)
    + scale_x_discrete(limits=SCENARIO_ORDER, expand=(0, 0.6, 0, 0.6))
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.1, 0))
    + guides(fill=False)
    + labs(x="", y="")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(10.5, 5.25),
        axis_text_y=element_blank(),
        axis_title_y=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_y=element_blank(),
        axis_line_y=element_blank(),
        axis_line_x=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_x=element_blank(),
        panel_grid=element_blank(),
    )
)

fig_combined = p_combined.draw()
fig_combined.canvas.draw()
ax_use, ax_bills = fig_combined.axes[0], fig_combined.axes[1]
import matplotlib.lines as mlines
_pt_l = fig_combined.transFigure.inverted().transform(ax_use.transData.transform((1, 1.0)))
_pt_r = fig_combined.transFigure.inverted().transform(ax_bills.transData.transform((1, 1.0)))
fig_combined.add_artist(mlines.Line2D(
    [_pt_l[0], _pt_r[0]], [_pt_l[1], _pt_r[1]],
    transform=fig_combined.transFigure,
    linestyle="dashed", color="#cccccc", linewidth=0.8, zorder=0.5, clip_on=False,
))
fig_combined
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-consumption-only-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual energy consumption before and after switching to a heat pump, for the gas-heated home at the statewide median energy bill (among gas-heated homes with full baseline cooling)."

fig_energy_only = p_combined.draw()
for a in list(fig_energy_only.artists):
    a.set_visible(False)
_ax_bills = fig_energy_only.axes[1]
for artist in _ax_bills.get_children():
    artist.set_visible(False)
_ax_bills.patch.set_visible(True)
_ax_bills.patch.set_facecolor("white")
_ax_bills.patch.set_edgecolor("none")
for child in fig_energy_only.get_children():
    if hasattr(child, "get_text") and "Annual energy bill" in getattr(child, "get_text", lambda: "")():
        child.set_visible(False)

_ax_use = fig_energy_only.axes[0]
_elec_norm_kwh = elec_kwh_after / norm_kwh
_gas_norm_kwh = gas_kwh_after / norm_kwh
for comp, bottom, height in [
    ("Electricity", 0, _elec_norm_kwh),
    ("Natural Gas", _elec_norm_kwh, _gas_norm_kwh),
]:
    _y = bottom + height / 2
    _c = COMP_LABEL_COLORS[comp]
    _ax_use.plot([SIDE_LINE_X, SIDE_LABEL_X - 0.02], [_y, _y],
                 color=_c, linewidth=0.8, clip_on=False)
    _ax_use.text(SIDE_LABEL_X, _y, comp,
                 color=_c, ha="left", va="center",
                 fontsize=8, fontweight="bold", clip_on=False)

fig_energy_only
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-consumption-elec-focus-state
#| fig-cap: "Electricity use and electric bills both grow roughly 3× after switching to a heat pump."

FOCUS_COMP_COLORS = {"Electricity": "#FFC729", "Natural Gas": "#CCCCCC"}
FOCUS_COMP_LABEL_COLORS = {"Electricity": "#FFC729", "Natural Gas": "#999999"}

_fc_rows: list[dict] = []
for scenario, ek, gk, eb, gb in [
    (SCENARIO_ORDER[0], elec_kwh_before, gas_kwh_before, _elec_before, _gas_before),
    (SCENARIO_ORDER[1], elec_kwh_after, gas_kwh_after, _elec_after, _gas_after),
]:
    _fc_rows.append({"panel": "Annual energy use", "scenario": scenario, "component": "Electricity",
                     "norm_value": ek / norm_kwh, "label": f"{ek:,.0f} kWh"})
    _fc_rows.append({"panel": "Annual energy use", "scenario": scenario, "component": "Natural Gas",
                     "norm_value": gk / norm_kwh, "label": f"{gk:,.0f} kWh"})
    _fc_rows.append({"panel": "Annual energy bill", "scenario": scenario, "component": "Electricity",
                     "norm_value": eb / norm_bill, "label": f"${eb:,.0f}"})
    _fc_rows.append({"panel": "Annual energy bill", "scenario": scenario, "component": "Natural Gas",
                     "norm_value": gb / norm_bill, "label": f"${gb:,.0f}"})

focus_combined_df = pl.DataFrame(_fc_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("panel").cast(pl.Enum(PANEL_ORDER)),
    pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)),
    pl.col("component").cast(pl.Enum(COMP_ORDER)),
)

focus_in_bar = focus_combined_df.filter(pl.col("norm_value") >= 0.08)

_ft_rows: list[dict] = []
for panel, norm, fmt in [
    ("Annual energy use", norm_kwh, lambda v: f"{v:,.0f} kWh"),
    ("Annual energy bill", norm_bill, lambda v: f"${v:,.0f}"),
]:
    p_df = focus_combined_df.filter(pl.col("panel") == panel)
    for s in SCENARIO_ORDER:
        nv = float(p_df.filter(pl.col("scenario") == s)["norm_value"].sum())
        _ft_rows.append({"panel": panel, "scenario": s,
                         "norm_value": nv, "label": fmt(nv * norm)})

focus_totals = pl.DataFrame(_ft_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("panel").cast(pl.Enum(PANEL_ORDER)),
    pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)),
)

_fsl_rows: list[dict] = []
for comp, bottom, height in [
    ("Electricity", 0, elec_kwh_after / norm_kwh),
    ("Natural Gas", elec_kwh_after / norm_kwh, gas_kwh_after / norm_kwh),
]:
    _fsl_rows.append({"panel": "Annual energy use", "y": bottom + height / 2,
                      "label_text": comp, "color": FOCUS_COMP_LABEL_COLORS[comp]})
for comp, bottom, height in [
    ("Electricity", 0, _elec_after / norm_bill),
    ("Natural Gas", _elec_after / norm_bill, _gas_after / norm_bill),
]:
    _fsl_rows.append({"panel": "Annual energy bill", "y": bottom + height / 2,
                      "label_text": comp, "color": FOCUS_COMP_LABEL_COLORS[comp]})

focus_side_labels_df = pl.DataFrame(_fsl_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("panel").cast(pl.Enum(PANEL_ORDER)),
)

p_focus_combined = (
    ggplot(focus_combined_df, aes(x="scenario", y="norm_value", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.6)
    + geom_text(
        focus_in_bar, aes(label="label"),
        position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
        color="white", fontweight="bold", size=10,
    )
    + geom_text(
        focus_totals, aes(x="scenario", y="norm_value", label="label"),
        va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold",
        size=10, nudge_y=0.02, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_segment(
        focus_side_labels_df,
        aes(x=SIDE_LINE_X, xend=SIDE_LABEL_X - 0.02, y="y", yend="y", color="color"),
        size=0.5, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_text(
        focus_side_labels_df,
        aes(x=SIDE_LABEL_X, y="y", label="label_text", color="color"),
        ha="left", va="center", size=8, fontweight="bold", inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + scale_color_identity()
    + facet_wrap("panel", ncol=2)
    + scale_fill_manual(values=FOCUS_COMP_COLORS)
    + scale_x_discrete(limits=SCENARIO_ORDER, expand=(0, 0.6, 0, 0.6))
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.1, 0))
    + guides(fill=False)
    + labs(x="", y="")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(10.5, 5.25),
        axis_text_y=element_blank(),
        axis_title_y=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_y=element_blank(),
        axis_line_y=element_blank(),
        axis_line_x=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_x=element_blank(),
        panel_grid=element_blank(),
    )
)

fig_focus = p_focus_combined.draw()
fig_focus.canvas.draw()
_ax_use, _ax_bills = fig_focus.axes[0], fig_focus.axes[1]

_pt_l = fig_focus.transFigure.inverted().transform(_ax_use.transData.transform((1, 1.0)))
_pt_r = fig_focus.transFigure.inverted().transform(_ax_bills.transData.transform((1, 1.0)))
fig_focus.add_artist(mlines.Line2D(
    [_pt_l[0], _pt_r[0]], [_pt_l[1], _pt_r[1]],
    transform=fig_focus.transFigure,
    linestyle="dashed", color="#cccccc", linewidth=0.8, zorder=0.5, clip_on=False,
))

_ann_color = "#333333"
_arrow_kw = dict(arrowstyle="<->", color=_ann_color, lw=1.0, shrinkA=0, shrinkB=0)
_dot_kw = dict(color=_ann_color, linewidth=0.5, linestyle="dotted", alpha=0.5)

elec_kwh_mult = elec_kwh_after / elec_kwh_before
_y_ref_use = elec_kwh_before / norm_kwh
_y_top_use = elec_kwh_after / norm_kwh
_ax_use.plot([1.3, 1.7], [_y_ref_use, _y_ref_use], **_dot_kw)
_ax_use.plot([1.3, 1.7], [_y_top_use, _y_top_use], **_dot_kw)
_ax_use.annotate("", xy=(1.5, _y_top_use), xytext=(1.5, 0), arrowprops=_arrow_kw)
_ax_use.text(1.53, _y_top_use / 2, f"{elec_kwh_mult:.1f}×",
             ha="left", va="center", fontsize=9, fontweight="bold", color=_ann_color)

elec_bill_mult = _elec_after / _elec_before
_y_ref_bill = _elec_before / norm_bill
_y_top_bill = _elec_after / norm_bill
_ax_bills.plot([1.3, 1.7], [_y_ref_bill, _y_ref_bill], **_dot_kw)
_ax_bills.plot([1.3, 1.7], [_y_top_bill, _y_top_bill], **_dot_kw)
_ax_bills.annotate("", xy=(1.5, _y_top_bill), xytext=(1.5, 0), arrowprops=_arrow_kw)
_ax_bills.text(1.53, _y_top_bill / 2, f"{elec_bill_mult:.1f}×",
               ha="left", va="center", fontsize=9, fontweight="bold", color=_ann_color)

report_vars["elec_kwh_multiplier"] = elec_kwh_mult
report_vars["elec_bill_multiplier"] = elec_bill_mult

fig_focus
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-components-decomposed-state
#| fig-cap: "Median energy bill components (decomposed) — Statewide"

p_decomposed = plot_bill_components_decomposed(
    median_current_state, median_hp_state,
    "Median energy bill components — Statewide",
)

fig_decomposed = p_decomposed.draw()
fig_decomposed.set_size_inches(10, 7)
fig_decomposed.canvas.draw()
_ax = fig_decomposed.axes[0]

_fixed_b = float(median_current_state["delivery_fixed"][0])
_vol_b = float(median_current_state["delivery_volumetric"][0])
_supply_b = float(median_current_state["supply"][0])
_fixed_a = float(median_hp_state["delivery_fixed"][0])
_vol_a = float(median_hp_state["delivery_volumetric"][0])
_supply_a = float(median_hp_state["supply"][0])

_del_before = _fixed_b + _vol_b
_del_after = _fixed_a + _vol_a
_del_mult = _del_after / _del_before
_sup_mult = _supply_a / _supply_b

_bar_hw = 0.3
_x_label = 1.5

_sup_color = "#E69F00"
_sup_mid_b = _del_before + _supply_b / 2
_sup_mid_a = _del_after + _supply_a / 2
_sup_label_y = (_sup_mid_b + _sup_mid_a) / 2

_ax.plot([1 + _bar_hw, _x_label], [_sup_mid_b, _sup_label_y],
         color=_sup_color, linewidth=0.7, alpha=0.6)
_ax.plot([2 - _bar_hw, _x_label], [_sup_mid_a, _sup_label_y],
         color=_sup_color, linewidth=0.7, alpha=0.6)
_ax.text(_x_label, _sup_label_y, f"×{_sup_mult:.1f}",
         ha="center", va="center", fontsize=9.5, fontweight="bold",
         color=_sup_color,
         bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.15", facecolor="white",
                   edgecolor="none", alpha=0.9))

_del_color = "#56B4E9"
_del_mid_b = _del_before / 2
_del_mid_a = _del_after / 2
_del_label_y = (_del_mid_b + _del_mid_a) / 2

_ax.plot([1 + _bar_hw, _x_label], [_del_mid_b, _del_label_y],
         color=_del_color, linewidth=0.7, alpha=0.6)
_ax.plot([2 - _bar_hw, _x_label], [_del_mid_a, _del_label_y],
         color=_del_color, linewidth=0.7, alpha=0.6)
_ax.text(_x_label, _del_label_y, f"×{_del_mult:.1f}",
         ha="center", va="center", fontsize=9.5, fontweight="bold",
         color=_del_color,
         bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.15", facecolor="white",
                   edgecolor="none", alpha=0.9))

report_vars["delivery_multiplier"] = _del_mult
report_vars["supply_multiplier"] = _sup_mult

_bar_edge = 2 + _bar_hw
_ax.spines["bottom"].set_bounds(_ax.get_xlim()[0], _bar_edge)
_g0 = _ax.get_ygridlines()[0]
_gc, _gw = _g0.get_color(), _g0.get_linewidth()
_ax.yaxis.grid(False)
for _yt in _ax.get_yticks():
    if 0 < _yt < _ax.get_ylim()[1]:
        _ax.hlines(_yt, _ax.get_xlim()[0], _bar_edge, colors=_gc, linewidths=_gw, zorder=0)
for _t in _ax.texts:
    if _t.get_position()[0] >= _bar_edge:
        _t.set_clip_on(False)

fig_decomposed
```

## Monthly consumption pattern

The same representative building's load curves, broken out by month. This
chart shows that the additional electricity use from heat pumps is concentrated
in winter — the season when the grid has the most spare capacity.

```{python}
#| label: load-monthly-consumption

_elec_col = "out.electricity.total.energy_consumption"
_before_monthly = df_before_load.select(
    pl.Series("month_label", [MONTH_LABELS[m - 1] for m in df_before_load["month"].to_list()]),
    pl.col(_elec_col).alias("elec_before"),
)
_after_monthly = df_after_load.select(
    pl.Series("month_label", [MONTH_LABELS[m - 1] for m in df_after_load["month"].to_list()]),
    pl.col(_elec_col).alias("elec_after"),
)
_monthly = _before_monthly.join(_after_monthly, on="month_label").with_columns(
    (pl.col("elec_after") - pl.col("elec_before")).clip(lower_bound=0).alias("elec_incremental"),
)

INCR_SEGMENT_ORDER = ["New electricity from heat pump", "Electricity before heat pump"]
incremental_df = pl.concat([
    _monthly.select("month_label", pl.col("elec_before").alias("kwh"))
    .with_columns(pl.lit("Electricity before heat pump").alias("segment")),
    _monthly.select("month_label", pl.col("elec_incremental").alias("kwh"))
    .with_columns(pl.lit("New electricity from heat pump").alias("segment")),
]).with_columns(
    pl.col("month_label").cast(pl.Enum(MONTH_LABELS)),
    pl.col("segment").cast(pl.Enum(INCR_SEGMENT_ORDER)),
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-consumption-before-after-state
#| fig-cap: "Monthly electricity consumption for a representative gas-heated home, before and after switching to a heat pump"

INCR_COLORS = {
    "Electricity before heat pump": "#C8A200",
    "New electricity from heat pump": "#FFC729",
}

_dec = _monthly.filter(pl.col("month_label") == "Dec")
_dec_before = float(_dec["elec_before"][0])
_dec_incr = float(_dec["elec_incremental"][0])
_n = len(MONTH_LABELS)
_lx = _n + 0.6

(
    ggplot(incremental_df, aes(x="month_label", y="kwh", fill="segment"))
    + geom_col(width=0.7)
    + scale_fill_manual(values=INCR_COLORS)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.05, 0))
    + scale_x_discrete(expand=(0.02, 0, 0.15, 0))
    + annotate("text", x=_lx, y=_dec_before / 2,
               label="Electricity use\nbefore heat pump",
               ha="left", va="center", size=9, color="#C8A200", fontweight="bold")
    + annotate("segment", x=_n + 0.15, xend=_lx - 0.05,
               y=_dec_before / 2, yend=_dec_before / 2, color="#C8A200", size=0.5)
    + annotate("text", x=_lx, y=_dec_before + _dec_incr / 2,
               label="New electricity use\nfrom heat pump",
               ha="left", va="center", size=9, color="#D4A800", fontweight="bold")
    + annotate("segment", x=_n + 0.15, xend=_lx - 0.05,
               y=_dec_before + _dec_incr / 2, yend=_dec_before + _dec_incr / 2,
               color="#D4A800", size=0.5)
    + labs(x="", y="Electricity consumption (kWh)")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(14, 5), legend_position="none")
)
```

## Bill change impact under default rates

How do total energy bills change when homes switch to heat pumps under today's
default flat rates? We compute the delta between each building's annual bill
before (run 1+2, current HVAC) and after (run 3+4, heat pump upgrade). The
`energy_total_bill` field includes electric, gas, oil, and propane costs.

```{python}
#| label: compute-bill-deltas

DELTA_SELECT = [
    BLDG_ID, "weight",
    pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_before"),
    "postprocess_group.has_hp",
    HEATING_TYPE_COL,
    "heats_with_natgas",
    "heats_with_oil",
    "heats_with_propane",
    "sb.electric_utility",
]

annual_current_state = (
    bills_current
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(DELTA_SELECT)
    .collect()
)
annual_hp_state = (
    bills_hp
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after"))
    .collect()
)
delta_state = annual_current_state.join(annual_hp_state, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
    (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
)
```

```{python}
#| label: histogram-helpers

BIN_WIDTH = 100

QUADRANT_COLORS = {
    "savings > $1k": "#1b5e20",
    "savings $0-1k": "#81c784",
    "losses $0-1k": "#ef9a9a",
    "losses > $1k": "#b71c1c",
}
QUADRANT_ORDER = list(QUADRANT_COLORS.keys())
QUADRANT_LABELS = {
    "losses > $1k": "LOSE > $1K",
    "losses $0-1k": "LOSE $0-1K",
    "savings $0-1k": "SAVE $0-1K",
    "savings > $1k": "SAVE > $1K",
}


def quadrant_pcts(df: pl.DataFrame) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Weighted percentage of homes in each bill-change quadrant."""
    total = df["weight"].sum()
    return {
        "savings > $1k": df.filter(pl.col("delta") < -1000)["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
        "savings $0-1k": df.filter((pl.col("delta") >= -1000) & (pl.col("delta") < 0))["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
        "losses $0-1k": df.filter((pl.col("delta") >= 0) & (pl.col("delta") < 1000))["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
        "losses > $1k": df.filter(pl.col("delta") >= 1000)["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
    }


def plot_quadrant_bar(
    rows: list[tuple[str, pl.DataFrame]],
    title: str = "How bills would change after switching to heat pumps:",
    title_parts: list[tuple[str, str]] | None = None,
) -> ggplot | Figure:
    """Horizontal stacked bar(s) showing quadrant proportions.

    *rows* is a list of ``(label, df)`` pairs where each *df* has ``delta``
    and ``weight`` columns. Each pair becomes one bar. Pass a single pair for
    one bar; pass multiple pairs for side-by-side comparison (e.g. different
    rate scenarios).

    When *title_parts* is given (a list of ``(text, color)`` tuples), the
    plotnine title is replaced by a matplotlib multi-color title and the
    function returns a ``Figure`` instead of a ``ggplot``.
    """
    records: list[dict] = []
    all_pcts: list[dict[str, float]] = []
    for row_label, df in rows:
        pct = quadrant_pcts(df)
        all_pcts.append(pct)
        for q in QUADRANT_ORDER:
            records.append({"scenario": row_label, "quadrant": q, "pct": pct[q]})

    scenario_order = [r[0] for r in reversed(rows)]
    plot_df = pl.DataFrame(records).with_columns(
        pl.col("quadrant").cast(pl.Enum(QUADRANT_ORDER)),
        pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(scenario_order)),
    )

    n_scenarios = len(rows)
    first_pct = all_pcts[0]
    stacking_order = list(reversed(QUADRANT_ORDER))
    extra_annotations: list = []

    cum = 0.0
    for q in stacking_order:
        mid = cum + first_pct[q] / 2
        cum += first_pct[q]
        if first_pct[q] < 2:
            continue
        extra_annotations.append(
            annotate(
                "text",
                x=n_scenarios + 0.45,
                y=mid,
                label=QUADRANT_LABELS[q],
                ha="center", va="bottom",
                size=8, color=QUADRANT_COLORS[q], fontweight="bold",
            )
        )

    if n_scenarios > 1:
        for i, (row_label, _) in enumerate(rows):
            bar_pos = n_scenarios - i
            extra_annotations.append(
                annotate(
                    "text",
                    x=bar_pos,
                    y=101,
                    label=row_label,
                    ha="left", va="center",
                    size=9, fontweight="bold",
                )
            )

    top_expand = 0.55 if n_scenarios > 1 else 1.2
    plotnine_title = "" if title_parts else title

    p = (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="scenario", y="pct", fill="quadrant"))
        + geom_col(position="stack", width=0.55)
        + geom_text(
            plot_df.filter(pl.col("pct") >= 3),
            aes(label="pct"),
            position=position_stack(vjust=0.5),
            format_string="{:.1f}%",
            color="white",
            size=10,
            fontweight="bold",
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=QUADRANT_COLORS, breaks=QUADRANT_ORDER)
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.15, 0))
        + scale_x_discrete(expand=(0, 0.35, 0, top_expand))
        + coord_flip()
        + guides(fill=False)
        + labs(x="", y="", title=plotnine_title)
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(
            figure_size=(12.5, max(2.6, 1.0 + 1.2 * n_scenarios)),
            axis_text=element_blank(),
            axis_ticks_x=element_blank(),
            axis_ticks_y=element_blank(),
            axis_line_x=element_blank(),
            axis_line_y=element_blank(),
            panel_grid=element_blank(),
            plot_title=(
                element_blank()
                if title_parts
                else element_text(ha="left", margin={"b": -8, "units": "pt"})
            ),
        )
    )
    for a in extra_annotations:
        p = p + a

    if title_parts:
        fig = p.draw()
        renderer = fig.canvas.get_renderer()
        fig_w_px = fig.get_window_extent().width

        _t1 = fig.text(0, -1, "xx", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", fontfamily="GT Planar")
        _t2 = fig.text(0, -1, "x x", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", fontfamily="GT Planar")
        space_fig = (
            _t2.get_window_extent(renderer).width
            - _t1.get_window_extent(renderer).width
        ) / fig_w_px
        _t1.remove()
        _t2.remove()

        x_pos = fig.axes[0].get_position().x0
        for i, (text_str, color) in enumerate(title_parts):
            t = fig.text(
                x_pos, 0.92, text_str.strip(),
                color=color, fontsize=12, fontweight="bold",
                fontfamily="GT Planar", va="bottom",
            )
            bb = t.get_window_extent(renderer=renderer)
            x_pos = bb.transformed(fig.transFigure.inverted()).x1
            if i < len(title_parts) - 1:
                x_pos += space_fig
        return fig

    return p


_QBU_BAR_HEIGHT = 0.45
_QBU_GROUP_GAP = 1.0
_QBU_INTRA_GAP = 0.12
_QBU_HEADER_SPACE = 0.55
_QBU_LABEL_SPACE = 0.35


def plot_quadrant_bar_by_utility(
    default_delta: pl.DataFrame,
    hprate_delta: pl.DataFrame,
    utility_names: dict[str, str],
    fuel_filter: pl.Expr,
    title_parts: list[tuple[str, str]],
    scenario_labels: tuple[str, str] = ("Default rate", "HP seasonal rate"),
) -> None:
    """Grouped quadrant bars for each utility in a single tall figure.

    *default_delta* and *hprate_delta* must have ``delta``, ``weight``, and
    ``sb.electric_utility`` columns. *fuel_filter* is applied to both.

    Displays as inline SVG via IPython so Quarto's ``embed`` shortcode can
    reference the cell output without hitting the raw-matplotlib Figure bug.
    """
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

    ordered_codes = list(reversed(UTIL_ORDER_CODES))

    util_data: dict[str, list[tuple[str, dict[str, float]]]] = {}
    for code in ordered_codes:
        name = utility_names[code]
        dd = default_delta.filter(fuel_filter & (pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == code))
        hd = hprate_delta.filter(fuel_filter & (pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == code))
        if dd.is_empty() or hd.is_empty():
            continue
        util_data[name] = [
            (scenario_labels[0], quadrant_pcts(dd)),
            (scenario_labels[1], quadrant_pcts(hd)),
        ]

    n_utils = len(util_data)
    n_scenarios = 2

    bar_positions: list[float] = []
    bar_scenario_labels: list[str] = []
    bar_pcts: list[dict[str, float]] = []
    utility_header_ys: list[tuple[float, str]] = []
    label_rows: list[tuple[float, dict[str, float]]] = []

    y = 0.0
    for u_idx, (util_name, scenarios) in enumerate(util_data.items()):
        utility_header_ys.append((y, util_name))
        y += _QBU_HEADER_SPACE

        first_pct = scenarios[0][1]
        label_rows.append((y, first_pct))
        y += _QBU_LABEL_SPACE

        for s_idx, (s_label, pct) in enumerate(scenarios):
            bar_positions.append(y)
            bar_scenario_labels.append(s_label)
            bar_pcts.append(pct)
            y += _QBU_BAR_HEIGHT + (_QBU_INTRA_GAP if s_idx < n_scenarios - 1 else 0)
        if u_idx < n_utils - 1:
            y += _QBU_GROUP_GAP

    y_max = y + 0.1
    fig_height = 1.5 + n_utils * 2.3

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12.5, fig_height))
    ax.set_xlim(0, 100)
    ax.set_ylim(-0.1, y_max)
    ax.invert_yaxis()
    ax.set_xticks([])
    ax.set_yticks([])
    for spine in ax.spines.values():
        spine.set_visible(False)

    stacking_order = list(reversed(QUADRANT_ORDER))

    for bp, pct in zip(bar_positions, bar_pcts):
        left = 0.0
        for q in stacking_order:
            width = pct[q]
            ax.barh(bp, width, left=left, height=_QBU_BAR_HEIGHT,
                    color=QUADRANT_COLORS[q], edgecolor="none", align="edge")
            if width >= 3:
                ax.text(left + width / 2, bp + _QBU_BAR_HEIGHT / 2, f"{width:.1f}%",
                        ha="center", va="center", color="white",
                        fontsize=10, fontweight="bold", fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans")
            left += width

    for bp, label in zip(bar_positions, bar_scenario_labels):
        ax.text(101, bp + _QBU_BAR_HEIGHT / 2, label,
                ha="left", va="center", fontsize=9, fontweight="bold",
                fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans")

    for header_y, util_name in utility_header_ys:
        ax.text(0, header_y, util_name,
                ha="left", va="top", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold",
                fontfamily="GT Planar", color="#333333")

    for label_y, first_pct in label_rows:
        cum = 0.0
        for q in stacking_order:
            mid = cum + first_pct[q] / 2
            cum += first_pct[q]
            if first_pct[q] < 2:
                continue
            ax.text(mid, label_y, QUADRANT_LABELS[q],
                    ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=8,
                    fontweight="bold", color=QUADRANT_COLORS[q],
                    fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans")

    fig.subplots_adjust(left=0.02, right=0.85, top=0.95, bottom=0.01)

    # Multi-color title
    renderer = fig.canvas.get_renderer()
    fig_w_px = fig.get_window_extent().width
    _t1 = fig.text(0, -1, "xx", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", fontfamily="GT Planar")
    _t2 = fig.text(0, -1, "x x", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", fontfamily="GT Planar")
    space_fig = (
        _t2.get_window_extent(renderer).width
        - _t1.get_window_extent(renderer).width
    ) / fig_w_px
    _t1.remove()
    _t2.remove()

    x_pos = fig.axes[0].get_position().x0
    for i, (text_str, color) in enumerate(title_parts):
        t = fig.text(
            x_pos, 0.97, text_str.strip(),
            color=color, fontsize=12, fontweight="bold",
            fontfamily="GT Planar", va="top",
        )
        bb = t.get_window_extent(renderer=renderer)
        x_pos = bb.transformed(fig.transFigure.inverted()).x1
        if i < len(title_parts) - 1:
            x_pos += space_fig

    display_svg(fig)
```

```{python}
#| label: elec-pct-change-stats

_cache_path = Path("../cache/annual_elec_kwh.parquet")
if not _cache_path.exists():
    _elec_col = "out.electricity.total.energy_consumption"
    _load_base = Path(f"/ebs/data/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2_sb/load_curve_monthly/state={STATE}")
    _before = (
        pl.scan_parquet(_load_base / "upgrade=00")
        .select("bldg_id", _elec_col)
        .group_by("bldg_id")
        .agg(pl.col(_elec_col).sum().alias("elec_kwh_before"))
        .collect()
    )
    _after = (
        pl.scan_parquet(_load_base / "upgrade=02")
        .select("bldg_id", _elec_col)
        .group_by("bldg_id")
        .agg(pl.col(_elec_col).sum().alias("elec_kwh_after"))
        .collect()
    )
    _annual_elec = _before.join(_after, on="bldg_id", how="inner")
    _cache_path.parent.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    _annual_elec.write_parquet(_cache_path)
else:
    _annual_elec = pl.read_parquet(_cache_path)

_COOLING_COL = "in.hvac_cooling_partial_space_conditioning"

_bill_meta = (
    bills_current
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(BLDG_ID, "heats_with_natgas", "heats_with_oil", "heats_with_propane",
            "sb.electric_utility", _COOLING_COL, "weight")
    .collect()
)

_fossil_elec_pct = (
    _annual_elec
    .join(_bill_meta, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner")
    .filter(
        pl.col("heats_with_natgas") | pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane")
    )
    .filter(pl.col("elec_kwh_before").abs() > 10)
    .with_columns(
        ((pl.col("elec_kwh_after") - pl.col("elec_kwh_before")) / pl.col("elec_kwh_before") * 100)
        .alias("pct_change"),
        pl.when(pl.col(_COOLING_COL) == "100% Conditioned").then(pl.lit("Full cooling"))
        .when(pl.col(_COOLING_COL) == "None").then(pl.lit("No cooling"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("Partial cooling"))
        .alias("cooling_group"),
        pl.col("sb.electric_utility").replace_strict(UTIL_NAMES).alias("utility_label"),
    )
)

_full_cooling = _fossil_elec_pct.filter(pl.col("cooling_group") == "Full cooling")
report_vars["elec_pct_change_full_cooling_p25"] = _weighted_percentile(_full_cooling, "pct_change", "weight", 0.25)
report_vars["elec_pct_change_full_cooling_p75"] = _weighted_percentile(_full_cooling, "pct_change", "weight", 0.75)

_cooling_order = ["Full cooling", "Partial cooling", "No cooling"]
_table_rows = []
for cg in _cooling_order:
    _cg_df = _fossil_elec_pct.filter(pl.col("cooling_group") == cg)
    for util in sorted(UTIL_NAMES.values()):
        _sub = _cg_df.filter(pl.col("utility_label") == util)
        if len(_sub) > 0:
            _table_rows.append({
                "cooling": cg,
                "utility": util,
                "median_pct_change": round(_weighted_percentile(_sub, "pct_change", "weight", 0.50)),
            })

_elec_change_table = pl.DataFrame(_table_rows).pivot(
    on="cooling", index="utility", values="median_pct_change",
).sort("utility")

```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-elec-pct-change

from lib.great_tables import get_switchbox_gt_tab_options
from lib.quarto import display_gt

_elec_change_gt = (
    GT(_elec_change_table, rowname_col="utility")
    .fmt_number(columns=_cooling_order, decimals=0, pattern="{x}%")
    .tab_spanner(label="Baseline cooling", columns=_cooling_order)
    .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options())
)
display_gt(_elec_change_gt)
```

### Statewide bill changes by fuel type

```{python}
#| label: compute-bill-deltas-by-fuel

non_hp_state = (
    delta_state
    .filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp") == False)
    .select("delta", "weight")
)

natgas_state = (
    delta_state
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)

_total_w = natgas_state["weight"].sum()
_save_w = natgas_state.filter(pl.col("delta") < 0)["weight"].sum()
_lose_1k_w = natgas_state.filter(pl.col("delta") > 1000)["weight"].sum()
report_vars["pct_natgas_save_default"] = _save_w / _total_w
report_vars["pct_natgas_lose_1k_default"] = _lose_1k_w / _total_w

oil_propane_state = (
    delta_state
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)

_op_total_w = oil_propane_state["weight"].sum()
_op_save_w = oil_propane_state.filter(pl.col("delta") < 0)["weight"].sum()
_op_save_1k_w = oil_propane_state.filter(pl.col("delta") < -1000)["weight"].sum()
_op_lose_1k_w = oil_propane_state.filter(pl.col("delta") > 1000)["weight"].sum()
report_vars["pct_oil_propane_save_default"] = _op_save_w / _op_total_w
report_vars["pct_oil_propane_save_1k_default"] = _op_save_1k_w / _op_total_w
report_vars["pct_oil_propane_lose_1k_default"] = _op_lose_1k_w / _op_total_w

elec_resistance_state = (
    delta_state
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")
    .select("delta", "weight")
)

_er_total_w = elec_resistance_state["weight"].sum()
_er_save_w = elec_resistance_state.filter(pl.col("delta") < 0)["weight"].sum()
_er_save_1k_w = elec_resistance_state.filter(pl.col("delta") < -1000)["weight"].sum()
_er_lose_1k_w = elec_resistance_state.filter(pl.col("delta") > 1000)["weight"].sum()
report_vars["pct_elec_resistance_save_default"] = _er_save_w / _er_total_w
report_vars["pct_elec_resistance_save_1k_default"] = _er_save_1k_w / _er_total_w
report_vars["pct_elec_resistance_lose_1k_default"] = _er_lose_1k_w / _er_total_w
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-histogram-natgas-state
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of annual bill change for natural gas-heated households switching to heat pumps (Statewide)"

_hist_df = natgas_state
_hist_snap = max(BIN_WIDTH, 50)
_hist_x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(_hist_df, "delta", "weight", 0.01) / _hist_snap) * _hist_snap
_hist_x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(_hist_df, "delta", "weight", 0.99) / _hist_snap) * _hist_snap
_hist_binned = (
    _hist_df.with_columns(
        ((pl.col("delta") / BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BIN_WIDTH + BIN_WIDTH / 2).alias("bin_center"),
    )
    .with_columns(
        pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") <= -1000).then(pl.lit("savings > $1k"))
        .when((pl.col("bin_center") > -1000) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 0)).then(pl.lit("savings $0-1k"))
        .when((pl.col("bin_center") >= 0) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 1000)).then(pl.lit("losses $0-1k"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("losses > $1k"))
        .alias("quadrant"),
    )
    .group_by("bin_center", "quadrant")
    .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
    .with_columns(pl.col("quadrant").cast(pl.Enum(QUADRANT_ORDER)))
)
(
    ggplot(_hist_binned, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="quadrant"))
    + geom_col(width=BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
    + geom_vline(xintercept=[-1000, 0, 1000], linetype="dotted", color="gray")
    + scale_fill_manual(values=QUADRANT_COLORS, breaks=QUADRANT_ORDER)
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(_hist_x_lo, _hist_x_hi))
    + labs(x="Annual Bill Change ($)", y="# of Homes (weighted)")
    + guides(fill=False)
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(10.5, 4.5))
)
```

### LMI scenarios (natgas, statewide)

We recompute the bill delta for natural gas households using the LMI-discounted bill columns. For non-LMI households
these columns equal the undiscounted values, so only LMI households' deltas shift.

```{python}
#| label: compute-lmi-bill-deltas-state

DELTA_META_COLS = [
    BLDG_ID, "weight", "postprocess_group.has_hp",
    HEATING_TYPE_COL, "heats_with_natgas", "heats_with_oil", "heats_with_propane",
]


def _lmi_delta_state(
    before_lf: pl.LazyFrame,
    after_lf: pl.LazyFrame,
    before_bill_cols: list[str],
    after_bill_cols: list[str],
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Compute statewide bill deltas using specified bill columns."""
    current = (
        before_lf
        .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .select(DELTA_META_COLS + before_bill_cols)
        .collect()
        .with_columns(
            pl.sum_horizontal(before_bill_cols).alias("bill_before"),
        )
    )
    after = (
        after_lf
        .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .select([BLDG_ID] + after_bill_cols)
        .collect()
        .with_columns(
            pl.sum_horizontal(after_bill_cols).alias("bill_after"),
        )
        .select(BLDG_ID, "bill_after")
    )
    return current.join(after, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
        (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
    )


delta_state_lmi40 = _lmi_delta_state(
    bills_current, bills_hp, LMI_40_BILL_COLS, LMI_40_BILL_COLS,
)
delta_state_lmi_full = _lmi_delta_state(
    bills_current, bills_hp, LMI_40_BILL_COLS, LMI_100_BILL_COLS,
)

natgas_state_lmi40 = (
    delta_state_lmi40
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
_lmi40_def_total = natgas_state_lmi40["weight"].sum()
_lmi40_def_save = natgas_state_lmi40.filter(pl.col("delta") < 0)["weight"].sum()
_lmi40_def_lose_1k = natgas_state_lmi40.filter(pl.col("delta") > 1000)["weight"].sum()
report_vars["pct_natgas_save_default_lmi40"] = _lmi40_def_save / _lmi40_def_total
report_vars["pct_natgas_lose_1k_default_lmi40"] = _lmi40_def_lose_1k / _lmi40_def_total

natgas_state_lmi_full = (
    delta_state_lmi_full
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-natgas-state-state-lmi-current
#| fig-cap: "Bill impact of switching to heat pumps for natural gas-heated households (Statewide)"

plot_quadrant_bar(
    [("Natural gas", natgas_state_lmi40)],
    title_parts=[
        ("How bills would change for ", "#000000"),
        ("natural gas", "#7A8A10"),
        (" heated homes after switching to ", "#000000"),
        ("heat pumps", "#c47600"),
        ("\n(with current LMI discounts)", "#000000"),
    ],
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-oil-propane-state
#| fig-cap: "Bill impact of switching to heat pumps for oil/propane-heated households (Statewide)"

plot_quadrant_bar(
    [("Oil / propane", oil_propane_state)],
    title_parts=[
        ("How bills would change for ", "#000000"),
        ("fuel oil", "#4a8fa0"),
        (" or ", "#000000"),
        ("propane", "#3a8fa0"),
        (" heated homes after switching to ", "#000000"),
        ("heat pumps", "#c47600"),
    ],
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-elec-resistance-state
#| fig-cap: "Bill impact of switching to heat pumps for electric resistance-heated households (Statewide)"

plot_quadrant_bar(
    [("Electric resistance", elec_resistance_state)],
    title_parts=[
        ("How bills would change for ", "#000000"),
        ("electric resistance", "#b08e00"),
        (" heated homes after switching to ", "#000000"),
        ("heat pumps", "#c47600"),
    ],
)
```

## Household counts by utility

Weighted number of households served by each NY electric utility,
from the statewide ResStock sample (upgrade 0 — current HVAC).

```{python}
#| label: fig-households-count-utility
#| fig-cap: "Number of households by electric utility"

_util_order_hh = [UTIL_NAMES[c] for c in UTIL_ORDER_CODES]
_n_hh = len(_util_order_hh)

_hh_rows: list[dict] = []
for _uc in UTIL_ORDER_CODES:
    _w = float(annual_current_state.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == _uc)["weight"].sum())
    _hh_rows.append({"utility": UTIL_NAMES[_uc], "households": _w})

_hh_df = pl.DataFrame(_hh_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(_util_order_hh)),
    (pl.col("households") / 1000).round(0).cast(pl.Int64).alias("households_k"),
)

_max_hh = _hh_df["households"].max()

_hh_annot: list = []
for _i, _label in enumerate(_util_order_hh):
    _row = _hh_df.filter(pl.col("utility") == _label)
    _count = _row["households"][0]
    _count_k = _row["households_k"][0]
    _hh_annot.append(
        annotate(
            "text", x=_i + 1, y=_count + _max_hh * 0.01, label=_label,
            ha="left", va="center",
            size=9, fontweight="bold",
        )
    )
    _hh_annot.append(
        annotate(
            "text", x=_i + 1, y=_max_hh * 0.02,
            label=f"{_count_k:,}k",
            ha="left", va="center",
            color="white", size=10, fontweight="bold",
        )
    )

_p_hh = (
    ggplot(_hh_df, aes(x="utility", y="households"))
    + geom_col(width=0.7, fill=SB_COLORS["sky"])
    + coord_flip()
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.15, 0))
    + scale_x_discrete(expand=(0, 0.35, 0, 1.0))
    + labs(x="", y="")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(13, max(3.0, 0.8 + 0.9 * _n_hh)),
        axis_text=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_x=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_y=element_blank(),
        axis_line_x=element_blank(),
        axis_line_y=element_blank(),
        panel_grid=element_blank(),
        legend_position="none",
    )
)
for _a in _hh_annot:
    _p_hh = _p_hh + _a
_p_hh
```

## Households by heating fuel type

Weighted distribution of households by primary heating fuel,
computed from the statewide ResStock sample (upgrade 0 — current HVAC).

```{python}
#| label: fig-households-by-fuel
#| fig-cap: "Percentage of households by primary heating fuel"

FUEL_CATEGORIES = [
    "Natural Gas", "Propane", "Fuel Oil", "Heat Pump", "Resistance", "Other/None",
]
FUEL_COLORS_BAR = {
    "Natural Gas": "#C8D66A",
    "Propane": "#68bed8",
    "Fuel Oil": "#4a8fa0",
    "Heat Pump": "#fc9706",
    "Resistance": "#FFC729",
    "Other/None": "#b0b0b0",
}
FUEL_LABEL_COLORS = {
    "Natural Gas": "#7A8A10",
    "Propane": "#3a8fa0",
    "Fuel Oil": "#4a8fa0",
    "Heat Pump": "#c47600",
    "Resistance": "#b08e00",
    "Other/None": "#666666",
}
FUEL_DISPLAY_NAMES = {
    "Natural Gas": "Natural Gas", "Propane": "Propane", "Fuel Oil": "Fuel Oil",
    "Heat Pump": "Heat Pump", "Resistance": "Resistance", "Other/None": "Other",
}

src = annual_current_state

total_w = src["weight"].sum()
fuel_weights = {
    "Natural Gas": float(src.filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))["weight"].sum()),
    "Fuel Oil": float(src.filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil"))["weight"].sum()),
    "Propane": float(src.filter(pl.col("heats_with_propane"))["weight"].sum()),
    "Resistance": float(
        src.filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")["weight"].sum()
    ),
    "Heat Pump": float(
        src.filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp"))["weight"].sum()
    ),
}
fuel_weights["Other/None"] = float(total_w - sum(fuel_weights.values()))

fuel_pcts = {k: round(v / total_w * 100) for k, v in fuel_weights.items()}

report_vars["pct_natgas_households"] = fuel_weights["Natural Gas"] / total_w

fuel_df = pl.DataFrame({
    "fuel": FUEL_CATEGORIES,
    "pct": [fuel_pcts[c] for c in FUEL_CATEGORIES],
}).with_columns(pl.col("fuel").cast(pl.Enum(FUEL_CATEGORIES)))

in_bar_labels = fuel_df.filter(pl.col("pct") >= 3)

cum = 0.0
fuel_annotations: list = []
for cat in FUEL_CATEGORIES:
    pct_val = fuel_pcts[cat]
    mid = cum + pct_val / 2
    cum += pct_val
    if pct_val < 2:
        continue
    fuel_annotations.append(
        annotate(
            "text", x=1.45, y=mid, label=FUEL_DISPLAY_NAMES[cat],
            ha="center", va="bottom",
            size=8, color=FUEL_LABEL_COLORS[cat], fontweight="bold",
        )
    )

p_fuel = (
    ggplot(fuel_df, aes(x="'Households'", y="pct", fill="fuel"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.8)
    + geom_text(
        in_bar_labels,
        aes(label="pct"),
        position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
        format_string="{:.0f}%",
        color="white", size=10, fontweight="bold",
    )
    + coord_flip()
    + scale_fill_manual(values=FUEL_COLORS_BAR, breaks=FUEL_CATEGORIES)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.02, 0))
    + scale_x_discrete(expand=(0, 0.35, 0, 1.2))
    + labs(x="", y="")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(10.5, 2.6),
        axis_text=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_x=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_y=element_blank(),
        axis_line_x=element_blank(),
        axis_line_y=element_blank(),
        panel_grid=element_blank(),
        legend_position="none",
    )
)
for a in fuel_annotations:
    p_fuel = p_fuel + a
p_fuel
```

### By utility

Same breakdown, one bar per utility.

```{python}
#| label: fig-households-by-fuel-utility
#| fig-cap: "Percentage of households by primary heating fuel, by utility"

_util_order = [UTIL_NAMES[c] for c in UTIL_ORDER_CODES]
_n_utils = len(_util_order)

_rows: list[dict] = []
_top_pcts: dict[str, float] = {}
for _uc in UTIL_ORDER_CODES:
    _sub = src.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == _uc)
    _tw = _sub["weight"].sum()
    _fw = {
        "Natural Gas": float(_sub.filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))["weight"].sum()),
        "Fuel Oil": float(_sub.filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil"))["weight"].sum()),
        "Propane": float(_sub.filter(pl.col("heats_with_propane"))["weight"].sum()),
        "Resistance": float(
            _sub.filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")["weight"].sum()
        ),
        "Heat Pump": float(
            _sub.filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp"))["weight"].sum()
        ),
    }
    _fw["Other/None"] = float(_tw - sum(_fw.values()))
    _pcts = {k: round(v / _tw * 100) for k, v in _fw.items()}
    _label = UTIL_NAMES[_uc]
    for cat in FUEL_CATEGORIES:
        _rows.append({"utility": _label, "fuel": cat, "pct": _pcts[cat]})
    if _label == _util_order[-1]:
        _top_pcts = _pcts

_fuel_util_df = pl.DataFrame(_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("fuel").cast(pl.Enum(FUEL_CATEGORIES)),
    pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(_util_order)),
)

_in_bar_util = _fuel_util_df.filter(pl.col("pct") >= 3)

_extra: list = []
_cum = 0.0
for cat in FUEL_CATEGORIES:
    _pv = _top_pcts[cat]
    _mid = _cum + _pv / 2
    _cum += _pv
    if _pv < 2:
        continue
    _extra.append(
        annotate(
            "text", x=_n_utils + 0.45, y=_mid,
            label=FUEL_DISPLAY_NAMES[cat],
            ha="center", va="bottom",
            size=8, color=FUEL_LABEL_COLORS[cat], fontweight="bold",
        )
    )

for _i, _label in enumerate(_util_order):
    _extra.append(
        annotate(
            "text", x=_i + 1, y=101, label=_label,
            ha="left", va="center",
            size=9, fontweight="bold",
        )
    )

_p = (
    ggplot(_fuel_util_df, aes(x="utility", y="pct", fill="fuel"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.7)
    + geom_text(
        _in_bar_util,
        aes(label="pct"),
        position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
        format_string="{:.0f}%",
        color="white", size=10, fontweight="bold",
    )
    + coord_flip()
    + scale_fill_manual(values=FUEL_COLORS_BAR, breaks=FUEL_CATEGORIES)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.15, 0))
    + scale_x_discrete(expand=(0, 0.35, 0, 1.0))
    + labs(x="", y="")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(13, max(3.0, 0.8 + 0.9 * _n_utils)),
        axis_text=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_x=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_y=element_blank(),
        axis_line_x=element_blank(),
        axis_line_y=element_blank(),
        panel_grid=element_blank(),
        legend_position="none",
    )
)
for _a in _extra:
    _p = _p + _a
_p
```

## Cross-subsidization under default rates

Are heat pump customers paying their fair share for delivery? The Bill
Alignment Test (BAT) answers this by comparing each customer's annual delivery
bill against their delivery cost-of-service (marginal cost + residual
allocation). A positive BAT means the customer overpays; a negative BAT means
they underpay.

We load the master BAT table (run 1+2, all buildings under current rates) and
find that heat pump customers systematically overpay for delivery, while fossil
fuel customers underpay — a cross-subsidy created by volumetric delivery rates.
The total BAT is the sum of delivery and supply components. The
unified histograms show the full population; the faceted versions break the
distribution down by heating type (fossil fuel, electric resistance, heat pump).

```{python}
#| label: bat-ingest

bat_baseline = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BAT_BASELINE, hive_partitioning=True)

bat_all = (
    bat_baseline
    .select(
        BLDG_ID, "weight",
        BAT_DELIVERY,
        BAT_SUPPLY,
        "economic_burden_delivery",
        RESIDUAL_DELIVERY,
        "annual_bill_delivery",
        HEATING_TYPE_COL,
        "heats_with_natgas", "heats_with_oil", "heats_with_propane",
        "sb.electric_utility",
    )
    .with_columns((pl.col(BAT_DELIVERY) + pl.col(BAT_SUPPLY)).alias(BAT_TOTAL))
    .collect()
)
```

### Cross-subsidy example: same home, before and after

To make the concept of bill alignment concrete before diving into the full distribution, we track the same median
gas-heated building we have been following throughout the report — before and after switching to a heat pump. For each
scenario, we compare what the home pays in delivery revenue against the delivery cost of serving it (marginal cost +
residual allocation).

```{python}
#| label: cross-subsidy-example-data

_bat_select = [
    BLDG_ID, "sb.electric_utility",
    "economic_burden_delivery",
    RESIDUAL_DELIVERY,
    "annual_bill_delivery",
    BAT_DELIVERY,
]
_bat_rename = {
    "economic_burden_delivery": "mc",
    RESIDUAL_DELIVERY: "residual",
    "annual_bill_delivery": "revenue",
}

median_gas_bat = (
    bat_baseline
    .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID) == median_bldg_id_state)
    .select(_bat_select)
    .collect()
    .rename(_bat_rename)
    .row(0, named=True)
)

bat_default_hp = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BAT_DEFAULT_HP, hive_partitioning=True)
_hp_raw = (
    bat_default_hp
    .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID) == median_bldg_id_state)
    .select(_bat_select)
    .collect()
    .rename(_bat_rename)
    .row(0, named=True)
)

# Use MC and bill from the HP run, but keep the per-customer residual from
# the gas run: one building switching doesn't change the utility-wide residual.
_hp_residual = median_gas_bat["residual"]
_hp_bat = _hp_raw["revenue"] - _hp_raw["mc"] - _hp_residual
median_hp_bat = {
    **_hp_raw,
    "residual": _hp_residual,
    BAT_DELIVERY: _hp_bat,
}

print(
    f"Median bldg {median_bldg_id_state} "
    f"({median_gas_bat['sb.electric_utility']}):"
)
for label, row in [("Gas", median_gas_bat), ("HP", median_hp_bat)]:
    print(
        f"  {label}: bill=${row['revenue']:,.0f}, "
        f"MC=${row['mc']:,.0f}, residual=${row['residual']:,.0f}, "
        f"BAT=${row[BAT_DELIVERY]:,.0f}"
    )
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-cross-subsidy-example
#| fig-cap: "Annual electric delivery bill vs. electric delivery cost of service for the median gas-heated home, before and after switching to a heat pump."

DECOMPOSE_COS = False

if DECOMPOSE_COS:
    CS_COMP_ORDER = [
        "Delivery Bill (Fixed)", "Delivery Bill (Volumetric)",
        "Marginal Cost", "Residual Cost",
    ]
    CS_COMP_COLORS = {
        "Delivery Bill (Fixed)": "#023047",
        "Delivery Bill (Volumetric)": "#56B4E9",
        "Marginal Cost": "#1b7837",
        "Residual Cost": "#a6dba0",
    }
    CS_LABEL_COLORS = {
        "Delivery Bill (Fixed)": "white",
        "Delivery Bill (Volumetric)": "white",
        "Marginal Cost": "white",
        "Residual Cost": "#333333",
    }
else:
    CS_COMP_ORDER = [
        "Delivery Bill (Fixed)", "Delivery Bill (Volumetric)",
        "Cost of Service",
    ]
    CS_COMP_COLORS = {
        "Delivery Bill (Fixed)": "#023047",
        "Delivery Bill (Volumetric)": "#56B4E9",
        "Cost of Service": "#a6dba0",
    }
    CS_LABEL_COLORS = {
        "Delivery Bill (Fixed)": "white",
        "Delivery Bill (Volumetric)": "white",
        "Cost of Service": "#333333",
    }
CS_BAR_ORDER = ["Cost of\nService", "Delivery\nBill"]

_SIDE_LINE_X = 2.35
_SIDE_TEXT_X = 2.45


def _bldg_records(bat_row: dict, bills_df: pl.DataFrame, scenario: str) -> list[dict]:
    fixed = float(bills_df["delivery_fixed"][0])
    vol = float(bills_df["delivery_volumetric"][0])
    records = [
        {"scenario": scenario, "bar": "Delivery\nBill",
         "component": "Delivery Bill (Fixed)", "dollars": fixed},
        {"scenario": scenario, "bar": "Delivery\nBill",
         "component": "Delivery Bill (Volumetric)", "dollars": vol},
    ]
    if DECOMPOSE_COS:
        records.extend([
            {"scenario": scenario, "bar": "Cost of\nService",
             "component": "Marginal Cost", "dollars": bat_row["mc"]},
            {"scenario": scenario, "bar": "Cost of\nService",
             "component": "Residual Cost", "dollars": bat_row["residual"]},
        ])
    else:
        records.append(
            {"scenario": scenario, "bar": "Cost of\nService",
             "component": "Cost of Service",
             "dollars": bat_row["mc"] + bat_row["residual"]},
        )
    return records


cs_records: list[dict] = []
cs_records.extend(_bldg_records(median_gas_bat, median_current_state, SCENARIO_ORDER[0]))
cs_records.extend(_bldg_records(median_hp_bat, median_hp_state, SCENARIO_ORDER[1]))

cs_plot_df = pl.DataFrame(cs_records).with_columns(
    pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)),
    pl.col("bar").cast(pl.Enum(CS_BAR_ORDER)),
    pl.col("component").cast(pl.Enum(CS_COMP_ORDER)),
)

cs_bar_totals = (
    cs_plot_df.group_by("scenario", "bar")
    .agg(pl.col("dollars").sum().alias("total"))
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)),
        pl.col("bar").cast(pl.Enum(CS_BAR_ORDER)),
        pl.col("total")
        .map_elements(lambda v: f"${v:,.0f}", return_dtype=pl.String)
        .alias("total_label"),
    )
)

cs_label_df = (
    cs_plot_df
    .sort("scenario", "bar", "component")
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("dollars").cum_sum().over("scenario", "bar").alias("_cum"),
    )
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("_cum") - pl.col("dollars") / 2).alias("y_mid"),
        pl.col("dollars")
        .map_elements(lambda v: f"${v:,.0f}", return_dtype=pl.String)
        .alias("dollar_label"),
        pl.col("component").cast(pl.String)
        .replace_strict(CS_LABEL_COLORS)
        .alias("label_color"),
    )
    .filter(pl.col("dollars") >= 50)
    .with_columns(pl.len().over("scenario", "bar").alias("_n"))
    .filter(pl.col("_n") > 1)
    .drop("_cum", "_n")
)

gas_bill = median_gas_bat["revenue"]
gas_cost = median_gas_bat["mc"] + median_gas_bat["residual"]
gas_bat = median_gas_bat[BAT_DELIVERY]

hp_bill_cs = median_hp_bat["revenue"]
hp_cost_cs = median_hp_bat["mc"] + median_hp_bat["residual"]
hp_bat_cs = median_hp_bat[BAT_DELIVERY]

gas_underpays = gas_bill < gas_cost
hp_overpays = hp_bill_cs > hp_cost_cs

bracket_df = pl.DataFrame([
    {"scenario": SCENARIO_ORDER[0], "x": 1.0, "xend": 2.0,
     "y": gas_cost, "yend": gas_bill,
     "clr": "#ffc729"},
    {"scenario": SCENARIO_ORDER[1], "x": 1.0, "xend": 2.0,
     "y": hp_cost_cs, "yend": hp_bill_cs,
     "clr": "#b71c1c" if hp_overpays else "#1b5e20"},
]).with_columns(pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)))

bat_labels = pl.DataFrame([
    {"scenario": SCENARIO_ORDER[0], "x": 1.5,
     "y": (gas_cost + gas_bill) / 2,
     "label": f"Underpays\n${abs(gas_cost - gas_bill):,.0f}/yr",
     "clr": "#ffc729"},
    {"scenario": SCENARIO_ORDER[1], "x": 1.5,
     "y": (hp_cost_cs + hp_bill_cs) / 2,
     "label": f"{'Overpays' if hp_overpays else 'Underpays'}\n${abs(hp_bat_cs):,.0f}/yr",
     "clr": "#b71c1c" if hp_overpays else "#1b5e20"},
]).with_columns(pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)))

report_vars["cs_gas_bill"] = gas_bill
report_vars["cs_gas_cost"] = gas_cost
report_vars["cs_gas_underpay"] = gas_cost - gas_bill
report_vars["cs_gas_bat"] = gas_bat
report_vars["cs_hp_bill"] = hp_bill_cs
report_vars["cs_hp_cost"] = hp_cost_cs
report_vars["cs_hp_overpay"] = hp_bill_cs - hp_cost_cs
report_vars["cs_hp_bat"] = hp_bat_cs

_hp_fixed = float(median_hp_state["delivery_fixed"][0])
_hp_vol = float(median_hp_state["delivery_volumetric"][0])

side_lines_df = pl.DataFrame([
    {"scenario": SCENARIO_ORDER[1], "x": _SIDE_LINE_X, "xend": _SIDE_TEXT_X - 0.03,
     "y": _hp_fixed / 2, "yend": _hp_fixed / 2},
    {"scenario": SCENARIO_ORDER[1], "x": _SIDE_LINE_X, "xend": _SIDE_TEXT_X - 0.03,
     "y": _hp_fixed + _hp_vol / 2, "yend": _hp_fixed + _hp_vol / 2},
]).with_columns(pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)))

side_text_df = pl.DataFrame([
    {"scenario": SCENARIO_ORDER[1], "x": _SIDE_TEXT_X,
     "y": _hp_fixed / 2, "label": "Electric Delivery\nBill (Fixed)",
     "clr": "#023047"},
    {"scenario": SCENARIO_ORDER[1], "x": _SIDE_TEXT_X,
     "y": _hp_fixed + _hp_vol / 2, "label": "Electric Delivery\nBill (Volumetric)",
     "clr": "#56B4E9"},
]).with_columns(pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)))

(
    ggplot(cs_plot_df, aes(x="bar", y="dollars", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.55)
    + geom_text(
        cs_label_df, aes(x="bar", y="y_mid", label="dollar_label", color="label_color"),
        fontweight="bold", size=9, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_text(
        cs_bar_totals, aes(x="bar", y="total", label="total_label"),
        va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold",
        size=9, nudge_y=40, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_segment(
        bracket_df,
        aes(x="x", xend="xend", y="y", yend="yend", color="clr"),
        size=1.2, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_text(
        bat_labels, aes(x="x", y="y", label="label", color="clr"),
        size=9, fontweight="bold", va="bottom", inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_segment(
        side_lines_df, aes(x="x", xend="xend", y="y", yend="yend"),
        color="#999999", size=0.5, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + geom_text(
        side_text_df, aes(x="x", y="y", label="label", color="clr"),
```
