```{python}
        ha="left", va="center", size=8, fontweight="bold", inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=CS_COMP_COLORS, breaks=CS_COMP_ORDER)
    + scale_color_identity()
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.15, 0))
    + scale_x_discrete(expand=(0, 0.5, 0, 0.9))
    + facet_wrap("scenario", ncol=2)
    + labs(
        x="", y="$ / year",
        title="Electric delivery bill vs. delivery cost of service — same home, before and after heat pump",
        fill="",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(10.5, 5.25), legend_position="none")
)
```

### Statewide summary and report statistics

We compute the mean delivery BAT by heating type — the key numbers that
quantify how much HP customers overpay and how much fossil fuel customers
underpay.

```{python}
#| label: bat-delivery-report-vars

BAT_BIN_WIDTH = 50
BAT_COLORS = {"overpaying": "#b71c1c", "underpaying": "#1b5e20"}

HEATING_TYPE_LABELS: dict[str, str] = {
    "fossil_fuel": "Fossil fuel",
    "electrical_resistance": "Electric resistance",
    "heat_pump": "Heat pump",
}
FACET_ORDER = ["Electric resistance", "Heat pump", "Fossil fuel", "Other"]

col = BAT_TOTAL
facet_dtype = pl.Enum(FACET_ORDER)

labeled_all = bat_all.with_columns(
    pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL)
    .replace_strict(HEATING_TYPE_LABELS, default=pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL))
    .fill_null("Other")
    .cast(facet_dtype)
    .alias("heating_label"),
)
total_weight = labeled_all["weight"].sum()

_hp_sub = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Heat pump")
_ff_sub = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Fossil fuel")

report_vars["hp_share_of_households"] = _hp_sub["weight"].sum() / total_weight
report_vars["hp_total_cross_subsidy_delivery_millions"] = (
    (_hp_sub[BAT_DELIVERY] * _hp_sub["weight"]).sum() / 1e6
)
report_vars["nonhp_households_millions"] = (total_weight - _hp_sub["weight"].sum()) / 1e6
report_vars["hp_mean_delivery_cost"] = (
    _weighted_mean(_hp_sub, "economic_burden_delivery", "weight")
    + _weighted_mean(_hp_sub, RESIDUAL_DELIVERY, "weight")
)
report_vars["ff_mean_delivery_cost"] = (
    _weighted_mean(_ff_sub, "economic_burden_delivery", "weight")
    + _weighted_mean(_ff_sub, RESIDUAL_DELIVERY, "weight")
)
report_vars["hp_mean_monthly_delivery_cost"] = report_vars["hp_mean_delivery_cost"] / 12
report_vars["ff_mean_monthly_delivery_cost"] = report_vars["ff_mean_delivery_cost"] / 12
report_vars["hp_mean_delivery_bill"] = _weighted_mean(_hp_sub, "annual_bill_delivery", "weight")
report_vars["ff_mean_delivery_bill"] = _weighted_mean(_ff_sub, "annual_bill_delivery", "weight")
report_vars["hp_mean_delivery_bat"] = _weighted_mean(_hp_sub, BAT_DELIVERY, "weight")

_bat_col = BAT_DELIVERY
_bat_simple_rows = []
for label, filt in [
    ("Heat pump", pl.col("heating_label") == "Heat pump"),
    ("Natural gas", pl.col("heats_with_natgas")),
    ("Oil / propane", pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane")),
    ("Electric resistance", pl.col("heating_label") == "Electric resistance"),
]:
    sub = labeled_all.filter(filt)
    if sub.is_empty():
        continue
    _bat_simple_rows.append({
        "heating_type": label,
        "mean_bat": _weighted_mean(sub, _bat_col, "weight"),
    })

_natgas_sub = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
_op_sub = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
_er_sub = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Electric resistance")
report_vars["ff_mean_delivery_bat"] = _weighted_mean(_ff_sub, _bat_col, "weight")
report_vars["natgas_mean_delivery_bat"] = _weighted_mean(_natgas_sub, _bat_col, "weight")
report_vars["oil_propane_mean_delivery_bat"] = _weighted_mean(_op_sub, _bat_col, "weight")
report_vars["elec_resistance_mean_delivery_bat"] = _weighted_mean(_er_sub, _bat_col, "weight")

```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-bat-simple
#| tbl-cap: "The average delivery over- or underpayment of different residential customer subclasses, relative to their delivery cost-of-service."
from lib.great_tables import get_switchbox_gt_tab_options
from lib.quarto import display_gt

_bat_simple_gt = (
    GT(pl.DataFrame(_bat_simple_rows), rowname_col="heating_type")
    .fmt_currency(columns="mean_bat", decimals=0, pattern="{x} / year")
    .cols_label(mean_bat="Average over- or underpayment")
    .tab_stubhead(label="Residential customer subclass")
    .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options())
)
display_gt(_bat_simple_gt)
```

```{python}
#| label: bat-by-utility-table

_util_col_order = list(reversed(UTIL_ORDER_CODES))

_bat_by_util_rows = []
for label, filt in [
    ("Heat pump", pl.col("heating_label") == "Heat pump"),
    ("Natural gas", pl.col("heats_with_natgas")),
    ("Oil / propane", pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane")),
    ("Electric resistance", pl.col("heating_label") == "Electric resistance"),
]:
    sub = labeled_all.filter(filt)
    if sub.is_empty():
        continue
    row: dict[str, object] = {"heating_type": label}
    for code in _util_col_order:
        util_sub = sub.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == code)
        if util_sub.is_empty():
            row[UTIL_SHORT[code]] = None
        else:
            row[UTIL_SHORT[code]] = _weighted_mean(util_sub, _bat_col, "weight")
    _bat_by_util_rows.append(row)

_util_cols = [UTIL_SHORT[c] for c in _util_col_order]
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-bat-by-utility

_bat_by_util_gt = (
    GT(pl.DataFrame(_bat_by_util_rows), rowname_col="heating_type")
    .fmt_currency(columns=_util_cols, decimals=0)
    .sub_missing(missing_text="—")
    .tab_header(title="Mean delivery over/underpayment by utility ($/yr)")
    .tab_source_note("Positive = overpaying; Negative = underpaying")
    .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options())
)
display_gt(_bat_by_util_gt)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-delivery-bat-by-heating-type
#| fig-cap: "Delivery bill alignment by heating type (Statewide)"

_DELIVERY_FACET_ORDER = ["Heat pump", "Electric resistance", "Natural gas", "Oil / propane"]
_delivery_facet_dtype = pl.Enum(_DELIVERY_FACET_ORDER)

_delivery_labeled = (
    labeled_all.with_columns(
        pl.when(pl.col("heats_with_natgas")).then(pl.lit("Natural gas"))
        .when(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane")).then(pl.lit("Oil / propane"))
        .otherwise(pl.col("heating_label").cast(pl.String))
        .alias("delivery_facet"),
    )
    .filter(pl.col("delivery_facet").is_in(_DELIVERY_FACET_ORDER))
    .with_columns(pl.col("delivery_facet").cast(_delivery_facet_dtype))
)

_d_col = BAT_DELIVERY
_d_x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(_delivery_labeled, _d_col, "weight", 0.01) / 500) * 500
_d_x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(_delivery_labeled, _d_col, "weight", 0.99) / 500) * 500

_d_binned = (
    _delivery_labeled.with_columns(
        ((pl.col(_d_col) / BAT_BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BAT_BIN_WIDTH + BAT_BIN_WIDTH / 2)
        .alias("bin_center"),
    )
    .with_columns(
        pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") < 0)
        .then(pl.lit("underpaying"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("overpaying"))
        .alias("sign"),
    )
    .group_by("bin_center", "sign", "delivery_facet")
    .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
)
_d_sign_dtype = pl.Enum(["underpaying", "overpaying"])
_d_plot_df = _d_binned.with_columns(pl.col("sign").cast(_d_sign_dtype))

_d_stat_rows: list[dict] = []
_d_median_rows: list[dict] = []
_d_mean_rows: list[dict] = []
for _ht in _DELIVERY_FACET_ORDER:
    _sub = _delivery_labeled.filter(pl.col("delivery_facet") == _ht)
    if _sub.is_empty():
        continue
    _tw = _sub["weight"].sum()
    _pct_over = _sub.filter(pl.col(_d_col) >= 0)["weight"].sum() / _tw * 100
    _w_mean = _weighted_mean(_sub, _d_col, "weight")
    _w_median = _weighted_percentile(_sub, _d_col, "weight", 0.5)
    _facet_bins = _d_plot_df.filter(pl.col("delivery_facet") == _ht)
    _y_max = float(_facet_bins["weight_sum"].max()) if not _facet_bins.is_empty() else 0.0
    _d_stat_rows.append({
        "delivery_facet": _ht,
        "x": _d_x_hi,
        "y": _y_max * 0.92,
        "label": f"median: ${_w_median:,.0f}  ·  mean: ${_w_mean:,.0f}\n{_pct_over:.0f}% overpaying",
    })
    _d_median_rows.append({"delivery_facet": _ht, "median_x": _w_median})
    _d_mean_rows.append({"delivery_facet": _ht, "mean_x": _w_mean})

_d_stats_df = pl.DataFrame(_d_stat_rows).with_columns(pl.col("delivery_facet").cast(_delivery_facet_dtype))
_d_medians_df = pl.DataFrame(_d_median_rows).with_columns(pl.col("delivery_facet").cast(_delivery_facet_dtype))
_d_means_df = pl.DataFrame(_d_mean_rows).with_columns(pl.col("delivery_facet").cast(_delivery_facet_dtype))

(
    ggplot(_d_plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="sign"))
    + geom_col(width=BAT_BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
    + geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="solid", color="gray", size=0.8)
    + geom_vline(
        data=_d_means_df, mapping=aes(xintercept="mean_x"),
        linetype="dashed", color="#333333", size=0.7,
    )
    + geom_vline(
        data=_d_medians_df, mapping=aes(xintercept="median_x"),
        linetype="solid", color="#333333", size=0.7,
    )
    + geom_text(
        _d_stats_df, aes(x="x", y="y", label="label"),
        ha="right", size=8, color="#333333",
        inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=BAT_COLORS)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.15, 0))
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(_d_x_lo, _d_x_hi))
    + facet_wrap("delivery_facet", ncol=1, scales="free_y")
    + labs(
        x="Delivery BAT per-customer ($/year)",
        y="# of Homes (weighted)",
    )
    + guides(fill=False)
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(10.5, 2.25 * 5))
)
```

```{python}
#| label: bat-dollar-totals

dollar_rows = []
for ht in FACET_ORDER:
    sub = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
    if sub.is_empty():
        continue
    weighted_bat = sub[col] * sub["weight"]
    total_over = weighted_bat.filter(weighted_bat >= 0).sum()
    total_under = weighted_bat.filter(weighted_bat < 0).sum()
    dollar_rows.append({
        "heating_type": ht,
        "total_overpayment": total_over,
        "total_underpayment": total_under,
        "net": total_over + total_under,
    })

(
    GT(pl.DataFrame(dollar_rows))
    .fmt_currency(columns=["total_overpayment", "total_underpayment", "net"], decimals=0)
    .cols_label(
        heating_type="Heating Type",
        total_overpayment="Total Overpayment",
        total_underpayment="Total Underpayment",
        net="Net",
    )
)
```

### Impact of tech-specific rates on fossil fuel cross-subsidization

What happens to fossil fuel customers' cross-subsidization if tech-specific rates zero out the net overpayment of HP
customers, ER customers, or both? We spread the released overpayment uniformly across fossil fuel households and report
the shift in their net, mean annual BAT, and mean monthly BAT (over a 6-month heating season).

```{python}
#| label: bat-scenario-table

HEATING_MONTHS = 6

ff = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Fossil fuel")
ff_weight = ff["weight"].sum()
ff_mean_before = _weighted_mean(ff, col, "weight")
ff_net_before = (ff[col] * ff["weight"]).sum()

er = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Electric resistance")
er_net = (er[col] * er["weight"]).sum()

hp = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Heat pump")
hp_net = (hp[col] * hp["weight"]).sum()

scenarios = [
    ("HP rate only", hp_net),
    ("ER rate only", er_net),
    ("HP + ER rates", hp_net + er_net),
]

scenario_rows = []
for name, released in scenarios:
    shift = released / ff_weight
    new_net = ff_net_before + released
    new_mean = ff_mean_before + shift
    scenario_rows.append({
        "scenario": name,
        "cross_subsidy_removed": released / 1e6,
        "ff_net_after": new_net / 1e6,
        "ff_mean_bat_before": ff_mean_before,
        "ff_mean_bat_after": new_mean,
        "monthly_heating": shift / HEATING_MONTHS,
    })

(
    GT(pl.DataFrame(scenario_rows))
    .fmt_currency(columns="cross_subsidy_removed", decimals=0, pattern="{x}M")
    .fmt_currency(columns="ff_net_after", decimals=0, pattern="{x}M")
    .fmt_currency(columns=["ff_mean_bat_before", "ff_mean_bat_after"], decimals=0, pattern="{x}/yr")
    .fmt_currency(columns="monthly_heating", decimals=0, pattern="{x}/mo")
    .cols_label(
        scenario="Scenario",
        cross_subsidy_removed="Cross-Subsidy Removed",
        ff_net_after="FF Net After",
        ff_mean_bat_before="FF Mean BAT Before",
        ff_mean_bat_after="FF Mean BAT After",
        monthly_heating="Monthly (Heating)",
    )
)
```

### Typical HP customer: bill vs. cost of service by utility

The single-utility example above is illustrative, but does the pattern hold statewide?
For each utility we pick the HP building nearest the weighted-median BAT and
compare its annual electric bill against its cost of service (marginal cost +
residual allocation). The overpayment gap is consistent across all seven
service territories.

```{python}
#| label: cross-subsidy-by-utility-data

hp_all_utils = (
    bat_baseline
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "heat_pump")
    .select(
        BLDG_ID, "weight", "sb.electric_utility",
        "economic_burden_delivery",
        RESIDUAL_DELIVERY,
        "annual_bill_delivery",
        BAT_DELIVERY,
    )
    .collect()
    .rename({
        "economic_burden_delivery": "mc",
        RESIDUAL_DELIVERY: "residual",
        "annual_bill_delivery": "revenue",
    })
)

utility_picks: list[pl.DataFrame] = []
for util_code in sorted(hp_all_utils["sb.electric_utility"].unique().to_list()):
    sub = hp_all_utils.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == util_code)
    if sub.is_empty():
        continue
    med = _weighted_percentile(sub, BAT_DELIVERY, "weight", 0.5)
    pick = (
        sub.with_columns((pl.col(BAT_DELIVERY) - med).abs().alias("_d"))
        .sort("_d").head(1)
    )
    utility_picks.append(pick)

median_hp_bldgs = pl.concat(utility_picks)

(
    GT(median_hp_bldgs.select(
        pl.col("sb.electric_utility").replace_strict(UTIL_DISPLAY).alias("utility"),
        "revenue", "mc", "residual", BAT_DELIVERY,
    ))
    .fmt_currency(
        columns=["revenue", "mc", "residual", BAT_DELIVERY],
        decimals=0,
    )
    .cols_label(
        utility="Utility",
        revenue="Delivery Bill",
        mc="Marginal Cost",
        residual="Residual Cost",
        **{BAT_DELIVERY: "BAT ($/yr)"},
    )
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-cross-subsidy-by-utility
#| fig-cap: "How much the typical HP customer overpays on delivery in each utility territory."

overpay_df = (
    median_hp_bldgs
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("revenue") - pl.col("mc") - pl.col("residual")).alias("delivery_overpayment"),
        pl.col("sb.electric_utility").replace_strict(UTIL_DISPLAY).alias("utility"),
    )
    .sort("delivery_overpayment", descending=True)
)

util_order = overpay_df["utility"].to_list()
overpay_df = overpay_df.with_columns(
    pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(util_order)),
    pl.col("delivery_overpayment")
    .map_elements(lambda v: f"${v:,.0f}/yr", return_dtype=pl.String)
    .alias("label"),
)

(
    ggplot(overpay_df, aes(x="utility", y="delivery_overpayment"))
    + geom_col(fill="#b71c1c", width=0.6)
    + geom_text(
        aes(label="label"),
        va="bottom", size=8, fontweight="bold", color="#b71c1c",
        nudge_y=20,
    )
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.12, 0))
    + labs(
        x="", y="$ / year",
        title="How much the typical HP customer overpays on delivery",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(10, 5),
    )
)
```


## Proposed fair rate: seasonal delivery pricing

We propose a simple seasonal delivery rate for heat pump customers: summer
rates match the current default, while winter rates are reduced to the level
where HP customers' annual delivery bills equal their cost-of-service, on
average. Each utility gets a unique winter rate reflecting its territory's
costs.

Both tariffs are calibrated URDB JSONs from the state's tariff config directory. The delivery component comes from the delivery-only file
(`*_calibrated.json`), and supply is the difference between the bundled file
(`*_supply_calibrated.json`) and delivery.

```{python}
#| label: rate-table-data

import json

TARIFF_DIR = f"rate_design/hp_rates/{_state_lower}/config/tariffs/electric"


def _load_rate(filename: str) -> dict:
    content = fetch_rdp_file(f"{TARIFF_DIR}/{filename}", ref=RDP_REF)
    return json.loads(content)["items"][0]


def _get_rates(tariff: dict) -> list[float]:
    return [tier[0]["rate"] for tier in tariff["energyratestructure"]]


summer_row: dict = {"metric": "Summer delivery rate (¢/kWh)"}
winter_row: dict = {"metric": "Winter delivery rate (¢/kWh)"}
discount_row: dict = {"metric": "Winter discount"}
for display, code in TARIFF_UTILS.items():
    seas_d = _load_rate(f"{code}_{TARIFF_SEASONAL}_calibrated.json")
    sd = _get_rates(seas_d)

    summer_row[display] = sd[0] * 100
    winter_row[display] = sd[1] * 100
    # Match delivery rows above: discount is winter vs summer *delivery* only, not bundled energy.
    discount_row[display] = (1 - sd[1] / sd[0]) * 100

rate_wide = pl.DataFrame([summer_row, winter_row, discount_row])
util_cols = [c for c in rate_wide.columns if c != "metric"]

```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-rate-table

_rate_gt = (
    GT(rate_wide, rowname_col="metric")
    .fmt_number(columns=util_cols, rows=pl.col("metric").is_in(["Summer delivery rate (¢/kWh)", "Winter delivery rate (¢/kWh)"]), decimals=2)
    .fmt(columns=util_cols, rows=pl.col("metric") == "Winter discount", fns=lambda x: f"{x:.1f}%")
    .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options())
)
display_gt(_rate_gt)
```

## Heat pump TOU rate structure

Beyond the flat seasonal rates above, we've also designed time-of-use (TOU) variants that align more closely with hourly
marginal costs. Each utility has a "paired" TOU tariff: a delivery-only file (`*_{TARIFF_TOU}_calibrated.json`) and a
delivery+supply file (`*_{TARIFF_TOU}_supply_calibrated.json`). We difference them to isolate the supply component.

All TOU tariffs use two seasons (winter: Oct–Mar, summer: Apr–Sep) with distinct on-peak and off-peak periods within
each season. Peak hours vary by utility, reflecting each utility's marginal cost profile.

```{python}
#| label: tou-rate-table-data

def _extract_peak_hours(schedule: list[list[int]], month_idx: int) -> str:
    """Return human-readable peak hours string for a given month in a URDB schedule."""
    row = schedule[month_idx]
    off_peak_period = row[0]
    peak_hours = [h for h, p in enumerate(row) if p != off_peak_period]
    if not peak_hours:
        return "None"
    start, end = peak_hours[0], peak_hours[-1] + 1

    def _fmt(h: int) -> str:
        if h in (0, 24):
            return "12am"
        if h == 12:
            return "12pm"
        return f"{h}am" if h < 12 else f"{h - 12}pm"

    return f"{_fmt(start)}–{_fmt(end)}"


rows: list[dict] = []
for display, code in TARIFF_UTILS.items():
    d = _load_rate(f"{code}_{TARIFF_TOU}_calibrated.json")
    b = _load_rate(f"{code}_{TARIFF_TOU}_supply_calibrated.json")

    dr = [tier[0]["rate"] for tier in d["energyratestructure"]]
    br = [tier[0]["rate"] for tier in b["energyratestructure"]]
    sr = [br[i] - dr[i] for i in range(4)]

    winter_peak = _extract_peak_hours(d["energyweekdayschedule"], 0)
    summer_peak = _extract_peak_hours(d["energyweekdayschedule"], 6)

    fixed = d["fixedchargefirstmeter"]

    rows.append({"utility": display, "season": "Winter", "peak_hours": winter_peak,
                 "delivery_off": dr[0] * 100, "delivery_on": dr[1] * 100,
                 "supply_off": sr[0] * 100, "supply_on": sr[1] * 100,
                 "total_off": br[0] * 100, "total_on": br[1] * 100,
                 "fixed_charge": fixed})
    rows.append({"utility": display, "season": "Summer", "peak_hours": summer_peak,
                 "delivery_off": dr[2] * 100, "delivery_on": dr[3] * 100,
                 "supply_off": sr[2] * 100, "supply_on": sr[3] * 100,
                 "total_off": br[2] * 100, "total_on": br[3] * 100,
                 "fixed_charge": fixed})

tou_df = pl.DataFrame(rows)

_coned_tou = tou_df.filter(pl.col("utility") == "ConEd")
for _row in _coned_tou.to_dicts():
    _sn = _row["season"].lower()
    report_vars[f"tou_coned_{_sn}_on"] = _row["total_on"]
    report_vars[f"tou_coned_{_sn}_off"] = _row["total_off"]

tou_df
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-tou-rate-table

rate_cols = ["delivery_off", "delivery_on", "supply_off", "supply_on", "total_off", "total_on"]

_tou_gt = (
    GT(tou_df, rowname_col="season", groupname_col="utility")
    .cols_label(
        peak_hours="Peak hours",
        delivery_off="Off-peak",
        delivery_on="On-peak",
        supply_off="Off-peak",
        supply_on="On-peak",
        total_off="Off-peak",
        total_on="On-peak",
        fixed_charge="Fixed charge",
    )
    .tab_spanner(label="Delivery (¢/kWh)", columns=["delivery_off", "delivery_on"])
    .tab_spanner(label="Supply (¢/kWh)", columns=["supply_off", "supply_on"])
    .tab_spanner(label="Total (¢/kWh)", columns=["total_off", "total_on"])
    .fmt_number(columns=rate_cols, decimals=1)
    .fmt_currency(columns="fixed_charge", decimals=2)
    .cols_align(align="center", columns=["peak_hours", *rate_cols, "fixed_charge"])
    .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options())
)
display_gt(_tou_gt)
```

### Marginal cost structure

To understand where these TOU windows and prices come from, we need to look at the underlying marginal costs that
vary hour by hour across the year. We load the four marginal cost 8760 traces that CAIRO uses for each utility —
energy, generation capacity, bulk transmission, and distribution + sub-transmission — from S3.

```{python}
#| label: mc-sources

MC_SOURCES: dict[str, dict] = {
    "mc_energy": {
        "path_template": "s3://data.sb/switchbox/marginal_costs/{state_lower}/supply/energy/utility={utility}/year={year}/data.parquet",
        "source_col": "energy_cost_enduse",
        "source_unit": "$/MWh",
    },
    "mc_gen_cap": {
        "path_template": "s3://data.sb/switchbox/marginal_costs/{state_lower}/supply/capacity/utility={utility}/year={year}/data.parquet",
        "source_col": "capacity_cost_enduse",
        "source_unit": "$/MWh",
    },
    "mc_bulk_tx": {
        "path_template": "s3://data.sb/switchbox/marginal_costs/{state_lower}/bulk_tx/utility={utility}/year={year}/data.parquet",
        "source_col": "bulk_tx_cost_enduse",
        "source_unit": "$/kWh",
    },
    "mc_dist_sub_tx": {
        "path_template": "s3://data.sb/switchbox/marginal_costs/{state_lower}/dist_and_sub_tx/utility={utility}/year={year}/data.parquet",
        "source_col": "mc_total_per_kwh",
        "source_unit": "$/kWh",
    },
}


def _load_mc(
    utility: str,
    mc_name: str,
    source: dict,
    year: int,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Load one MC parquet for one utility, validate, and normalize to $/kWh."""
    path = source["path_template"].format(utility=utility, year=year, state_lower=_state_lower)
    df = pl.read_parquet(path, storage_options={"aws_region": "us-west-2"})

    assert df.height == 8760, f"{mc_name} for {utility}: expected 8760 rows, got {df.height}"
    assert df[source["source_col"]].null_count() == 0, f"{mc_name} for {utility}: nulls in {source['source_col']}"

    result = df.select(pl.col("timestamp"), pl.col(source["source_col"]).alias(mc_name))
    if source["source_unit"] == "$/MWh":
        result = result.with_columns(pl.col(mc_name) / 1000)
    return result
```

```{python}
#| label: load-all-mcs

_utility_codes = list(TARIFF_UTILS.values())
_mc_frames: list[pl.DataFrame] = []

for _u in _utility_codes:
    _mc_dfs = [_load_mc(_u, mc_name, source, YEAR) for mc_name, source in MC_SOURCES.items()]
    _combined = _mc_dfs[0]
    for _mc_df in _mc_dfs[1:]:
        _combined = _combined.join(_mc_df, on="timestamp", how="inner")
    assert _combined.height == 8760, f"{_u}: joined MC has {_combined.height} rows, expected 8760"
    _mc_frames.append(_combined.with_columns(pl.lit(_u).alias("utility")))

mc_all = pl.concat(_mc_frames)
print(f"Loaded marginal costs: {mc_all.height} rows, {mc_all.width} columns, {len(_utility_codes)} utilities")
```

### Representative-day algorithm

To visualize how marginal costs shape the TOU rate, we select four **representative days** per utility: a low-cost
and high-cost day in each of summer and winter. For each group, we compute the mean hourly profile — the average
value of each MC at each hour — which smooths daily noise while preserving the characteristic shape.

```{python}
#| label: representative-day-algorithm

SUMMER_MONTHS = [6, 7, 8]
WINTER_MONTHS = [12, 1, 2]

MC_COLS = ["mc_energy", "mc_gen_cap", "mc_bulk_tx", "mc_dist_sub_tx"]

MC_DISPLAY_LABELS = {
    "mc_energy": "Energy",
    "mc_gen_cap": "Gen Capacity",
    "mc_bulk_tx": "Bulk Tx",
    "mc_dist_sub_tx": "Dist+Sub-Tx",
}

MC_ORDER = list(reversed(MC_DISPLAY_LABELS.values()))
MC_ENUM = pl.Enum(MC_ORDER)

SEASON_ENUM = pl.Enum(["Summer", "Winter"])
CAP_ENUM = pl.Enum(["Low-cost Day", "High-cost Day"])

MC_DISPLAY_COLORS = {
    "Energy": SB_COLORS["carrot"],
    "Gen Capacity": SB_COLORS["saffron"],
    "Bulk Tx": SB_COLORS["sky"],
    "Dist+Sub-Tx": SB_COLORS["midnight"],
}


def _select_representative_days(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Build mean hourly profiles for 4 representative day types.

    Returns a long-format DataFrame with columns:
    season, cap_level, hour (1-24), mc_type, value ($/kWh).
    """
    enriched = df.with_columns(
        pl.col("timestamp").dt.month().alias("month"),
        pl.col("timestamp").dt.date().alias("date"),
        pl.col("timestamp").dt.hour().alias("hour"),
    )

    daily_cap = enriched.group_by("date", "month").agg(
        (pl.col("mc_gen_cap") + pl.col("mc_bulk_tx")).sum().alias("daily_cap_cost")
    )

    results: list[pl.DataFrame] = []
    for season_name, months in [("Summer", SUMMER_MONTHS), ("Winter", WINTER_MONTHS)]:
        season_days = daily_cap.filter(pl.col("month").is_in(months))
        median_cap = season_days["daily_cap_cost"].median()

        low_dates = season_days.filter(pl.col("daily_cap_cost") <= median_cap)["date"]
        high_dates = season_days.filter(pl.col("daily_cap_cost") > median_cap)["date"]

        for level_name, dates in [("Low-cost Day", low_dates), ("High-cost Day", high_dates)]:
            group = enriched.filter(pl.col("date").is_in(dates))
            hourly_mean = (
                group.group_by("hour")
                .agg([pl.col(c).mean() for c in MC_COLS])
                .sort("hour")
                .with_columns(pl.col("hour") + 1)
            )
            long = (
                hourly_mean.unpivot(index=["hour"], variable_name="mc_type", value_name="value")
                .with_columns(
                    pl.col("mc_type").replace_strict(MC_DISPLAY_LABELS).cast(MC_ENUM),
                    pl.lit(season_name).cast(SEASON_ENUM).alias("season"),
                    pl.lit(level_name).cast(CAP_ENUM).alias("cap_level"),
                )
            )
            results.append(long)

    return pl.concat(results)
```

### TOU rate overlay

We overlay the calibrated TOU rate on the representative-day charts so the reader can see how the two-period
rate approximates the underlying cost shape. The `_load_tou_profile` function reuses the `_load_rate` helper
already defined above.

```{python}
#| label: tou-overlay-helpers

def _hourly_tou_profile(tariff: dict, schedule: list[list[int]]) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Build a season x hour DataFrame of rates ($/kWh) from a URDB tariff."""
    rates = [tier[0]["rate"] for tier in tariff["energyratestructure"]]
    rows: list[dict] = []
    for season_name, month_idx, off_idx, on_idx in [
        ("Summer", 6, 2, 3),
        ("Winter", 0, 0, 1),
    ]:
        row = schedule[month_idx]
        off_peak_period = row[0]
        for h in range(24):
            idx = off_idx if row[h] == off_peak_period else on_idx
            rows.append({"season": season_name, "hour": h + 1, "tou_rate": rates[idx]})
    return pl.DataFrame(rows).with_columns(pl.col("season").cast(SEASON_ENUM))


def _load_tou_profiles(
    utility_code: str,
) -> tuple[pl.DataFrame, pl.DataFrame, pl.DataFrame]:
    """Load TOU rate profiles for a utility: (bundled_cal, supply_cal, supply_precal)."""
    d = _load_rate(f"{utility_code}_{TARIFF_TOU}_calibrated.json")
    b = _load_rate(f"{utility_code}_{TARIFF_TOU}_supply_calibrated.json")
    pre = _load_rate(f"{utility_code}_{TARIFF_TOU}_supply.json")

    schedule = d["energyweekdayschedule"]
    bundled_cal = _hourly_tou_profile(b, schedule)
    delivery_cal = _hourly_tou_profile(d, schedule)
    supply_cal = bundled_cal.with_columns(
        (pl.col("tou_rate") - delivery_cal["tou_rate"]).alias("tou_rate")
    )
    supply_precal = _hourly_tou_profile(pre, schedule)

    return bundled_cal, supply_cal, supply_precal


def _expand_tou_to_facets(tou: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Replicate a season x hour TOU DataFrame across both cap_level facets."""
    return pl.concat([
        tou.with_columns(pl.lit(level).cast(CAP_ENUM).alias("cap_level"))
        for level in CAP_ENUM.categories
    ])


def _plot_representative_days(
    utility: str,
    tou_rates: pl.DataFrame | None = None,
    season: str = "both",
) -> None:
    """Plot faceted stacked bar chart of representative day MCs for one utility.

    *season*: "both" (2x2 grid), "Summer", or "Winter" (1x2 strip).
    Optionally overlays the calibrated bundled TOU rate as a solid black step line.
    """
    name = UTIL_NAMES.get(utility, utility)
    rep = _select_representative_days(mc_all.filter(pl.col("utility") == utility))
    rep = rep.with_columns((pl.col("hour") + 0.5).alias("hour"))

    if season != "both":
        rep = rep.filter(pl.col("season") == season)

    season_label = f" — {season}" if season != "both" else ""
    fig_height = 7 if season == "both" else 4
    p = (
        ggplot(rep, aes(x="hour", y="value", fill="mc_type"))
        + geom_col(width=1.0)
        + facet_grid("season", "cap_level")
        + scale_fill_manual(values=MC_DISPLAY_COLORS)
        + scale_x_continuous(breaks=[1, 6, 12, 18, 24])
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.05, 0))
        + labs(
            title=f"{name}:{season_label} Time-of-use Prices vs. Marginal Costs ($/kWh, {YEAR})",
            x="Hour of Day",
            y="$/kWh",
            fill="",
        )
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(figure_size=(10.5, fig_height))
    )

    if tou_rates is not None:
        tou_data = tou_rates if season == "both" else tou_rates.filter(pl.col("season") == season)
        p = p + geom_step(
            _expand_tou_to_facets(tou_data),
            aes(x="hour", y="tou_rate"),
            inherit_aes=False,
            direction="hv",
            color="black",
            size=0.7,
            linetype="solid",
        )

        tou_lbl = tou_data
        label_rows: list[dict] = []
        for s in tou_lbl["season"].unique().to_list():
            s_data = tou_lbl.filter(pl.col("season") == s).sort("hour")
            rates_sorted = s_data["tou_rate"].unique().sort().to_list()
            off_rate, on_rate = rates_sorted[0], rates_sorted[-1]
            off_hours = s_data.filter(pl.col("tou_rate") == off_rate)["hour"].to_list()
            on_hours = s_data.filter(pl.col("tou_rate") == on_rate)["hour"].to_list()
            label_rows.append({"season": s, "hour": float(off_hours[len(off_hours) // 4]), "y": off_rate, "label": f"Off-peak: ${off_rate:.3f}"})
            label_rows.append({"season": s, "hour": float(on_hours[len(on_hours) // 2]), "y": on_rate, "label": f"On-peak: ${on_rate:.3f}"})
        labels_df = pl.DataFrame(label_rows).with_columns(
            pl.col("season").cast(SEASON_ENUM),
            pl.lit("Low-cost Day").cast(CAP_ENUM).alias("cap_level"),
        )
        p = p + geom_text(
            labels_df, aes(x="hour", y="y", label="label"),
            inherit_aes=False, va="bottom", nudge_y=0.002, size=9, color="black",
        )

    p.show()
```

### Central Hudson: TOU rate vs. marginal costs

We start with Central Hudson to illustrate how TOU windows and prices are derived from marginal costs.

```{python}
#| label: fig-rep-days-cenhud-summer
#| fig-cap: "Central Hudson summer: hourly marginal costs (stacked bars) vs. calibrated TOU rate (black line)"

_cenhud_bundled, _, _ = _load_tou_profiles("cenhud")
_plot_representative_days("cenhud", tou_rates=_cenhud_bundled, season="Summer")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rep-days-cenhud
#| fig-cap: "Central Hudson summer and winter: hourly marginal costs (stacked bars) vs. calibrated TOU rate (black line)"

_plot_representative_days("cenhud", tou_rates=_cenhud_bundled, season="both")
```

### TOU report variables

We extract key stats from the Central Hudson TOU rate and marginal cost structure for use in the report narrative.

```{python}
#| label: tou-report-vars

def _tou_season_stats(tou: pl.DataFrame, season: str) -> dict:
    """Extract on/off-peak hours and rates for one season of a TOU profile."""
    s = tou.filter(pl.col("season") == season).sort("hour")
    rates = s["tou_rate"].unique().sort().to_list()
    off_rate, on_rate = rates[0], rates[-1]
    off_hours = s.filter(pl.col("tou_rate") == off_rate)["hour"].to_list()
    on_hours = s.filter(pl.col("tou_rate") == on_rate)["hour"].to_list()
    return {
        "off_rate": off_rate,
        "on_rate": on_rate,
        "off_hours": off_hours,
        "on_hours": on_hours,
        "on_duration": len(on_hours),
        "on_start": on_hours[0],
        "ratio": on_rate / off_rate,
    }


def _rep_day_mc_stats(utility: str) -> dict:
    """Compute average hourly MC stats for each representative day type."""
    rep = _select_representative_days(mc_all.filter(pl.col("utility") == utility))
    stats: dict = {}
    for season in ["Summer", "Winter"]:
        for cap_level in ["Low-cost Day", "High-cost Day"]:
            subset = rep.filter(
                (pl.col("season") == season) & (pl.col("cap_level") == cap_level)
            )
            tag = f"{season.lower()}_{cap_level.split('-')[0].lower()}"
            energy = subset.filter(pl.col("mc_type") == "Energy")["value"]
            cap_types = ["Gen Capacity", "Bulk Tx", "Dist+Sub-Tx"]
            cap = subset.filter(pl.col("mc_type").is_in(cap_types))
            cap_hourly = cap.group_by("hour").agg(pl.col("value").sum())["value"]
            stats[f"{tag}_energy_avg"] = energy.mean()
            stats[f"{tag}_energy_max"] = energy.max()
            stats[f"{tag}_cap_avg"] = cap_hourly.mean()
            stats[f"{tag}_cap_min"] = cap_hourly.min()
            stats[f"{tag}_cap_max"] = cap_hourly.max()
    return stats


for _season in ["Summer", "Winter"]:
    _stats = _tou_season_stats(_cenhud_bundled, _season)
    _prefix = f"cenhud_{_season.lower()}"
    for _k, _val in _stats.items():
        report_vars[f"{_prefix}_{_k}"] = _val

_cenhud_mc = _rep_day_mc_stats("cenhud")
for _k, _val in _cenhud_mc.items():
    report_vars[f"cenhud_{_k}"] = _val

report_vars["cenhud_winter_start_later"] = (
    report_vars["cenhud_winter_on_start"] - report_vars["cenhud_summer_on_start"]
)
report_vars["cenhud_offpeak_winter_minus_summer"] = (
    report_vars["cenhud_winter_off_rate"] - report_vars["cenhud_summer_off_rate"]
)
report_vars["cenhud_onpeak_winter_minus_summer"] = (
    report_vars["cenhud_winter_on_rate"] - report_vars["cenhud_summer_on_rate"]
)
```

### TOU schedule charts

The charts below visualize these TOU schedules across the 24-hour day, with delivery and supply stacked so the reader
can see each component's contribution. Each utility gets its own chart with Summer and Winter panels (shared y-axis so
seasonal differences are visually apparent); the height of each block represents the total volumetric rate during that
period.

```{python}
#| label: tou-schedule-charts-setup

import io

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

_HOUR_TICKS = list(range(0, 25, 3))
_HOUR_LABELS = ["12am", "3am", "6am", "9am", "12pm", "3pm", "6pm", "9pm", "12am"]
_C_DELIVERY = SB_COLORS["midnight"]
_C_SUPPLY = SB_COLORS["sky"]
_FLAT_DEFAULT_UTILS = {"nimo", "nyseg", "cenhud", "rge"}


def _extract_season_data(d, b, month_idx, off_idx, on_idx):
    """Extract peak hours and rates (in ¢/kWh) for one season."""
    dr = [t[0]["rate"] for t in d["energyratestructure"]]
    br = [t[0]["rate"] for t in b["energyratestructure"]]
    sr = [br[i] - dr[i] for i in range(len(dr))]

    row = d["energyweekdayschedule"][month_idx]
    off_p = row[0]
    pks = [h for h, p in enumerate(row) if p != off_p]
    ps, pe = pks[0], pks[-1] + 1

    do, dn = dr[off_idx] * 100, dr[on_idx] * 100
    so, sn = sr[off_idx] * 100, sr[on_idx] * 100
    return ps, pe, do, so, dn, sn


def _draw_tou_season(ax, season_title, ps, pe, do, so, dn, sn, ymax):
    """Draw stacked delivery+supply rectangles for one season on a matplotlib Axes."""
    to, tn = do + so, dn + sn

    segments = [
        (0, ps, do, so, to, True),
        (ps, pe, dn, sn, tn, True),
        (pe, 24, do, so, to, False),  # trailing off-peak: no redundant labels
    ]
    for xmin, xmax, dv, sv, tv, show_label in segments:
        ax.add_patch(plt.Rectangle((xmin, 0), xmax - xmin, dv,
                                    facecolor=_C_DELIVERY, edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.8))
        ax.add_patch(plt.Rectangle((xmin, dv), xmax - xmin, sv,
                                    facecolor=_C_SUPPLY, edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.8))
        if show_label:
            cx = (xmin + xmax) / 2
            ax.text(cx, tv + 0.3, f"{tv:.0f}¢",
                    ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=9, fontweight="bold", color="#333333")
            if dv >= 1.5:
                ax.text(cx, dv / 2, f"{dv:.0f}¢",
                        ha="center", va="center", fontsize=8, fontweight="bold", color="white")
            if sv >= 1.5:
                ax.text(cx, dv + sv / 2, f"{sv:.0f}¢",
                        ha="center", va="center", fontsize=8, fontweight="bold", color="white")

    ax.set_xlim(0, 24)
    ax.set_ylim(0, ymax * 1.15)
    ax.set_xticks(_HOUR_TICKS)
    ax.set_xticklabels(_HOUR_LABELS, fontsize=8)
    ax.set_ylabel("¢/kWh", fontsize=9)
    ax.set_title(season_title, fontsize=11, fontweight="bold", loc="left")
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.3)
    ax.set_axisbelow(True)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)


def _make_tou_chart(display_name, tariff_code):
    """Create a 2-panel (Summer on top, Winter on bottom) TOU schedule chart."""
    d = _load_rate(f"{tariff_code}_{TARIFF_TOU}_calibrated.json")
    b = _load_rate(f"{tariff_code}_{TARIFF_TOU}_supply_calibrated.json")

    w_ps, w_pe, w_do, w_so, w_dn, w_sn = _extract_season_data(d, b, 0, 0, 1)
    s_ps, s_pe, s_do, s_so, s_dn, s_sn = _extract_season_data(d, b, 6, 2, 3)
    ymax = max(w_do + w_so, w_dn + w_sn, s_do + s_so, s_dn + s_sn)

    fig, (ax_top, ax_bot) = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(7, 3.5), constrained_layout=True)
    fig.suptitle(display_name, fontsize=13, fontweight="bold")
    _draw_tou_season(ax_top, "Summer", s_ps, s_pe, s_do, s_so, s_dn, s_sn, ymax)
    _draw_tou_season(ax_bot, "Winter", w_ps, w_pe, w_do, w_so, w_dn, w_sn, ymax)

    if tariff_code in _FLAT_DEFAULT_UTILS:
        default_b = _load_rate(f"{tariff_code}_default_supply_calibrated.json")
        default_rates = _get_rates(default_b)

        w_period = default_b["energyweekdayschedule"][0][0]
        w_default = default_rates[w_period] * 100

        s_period = default_b["energyweekdayschedule"][6][0]
        s_default = default_rates[s_period] * 100

        for ax, rate in [(ax_top, s_default), (ax_bot, w_default)]:
            ax.axhline(rate, color=SB_COLORS["saffron"], linestyle="--",
                       linewidth=1.0, zorder=0)
            ax.text(24, rate, f"  {rate:.0f}¢ default",
                    va="center", ha="left", fontsize=8,
                    color=SB_COLORS["saffron"], fontweight="bold",
                    clip_on=False)

    delivery_patch = mpatches.Patch(facecolor=_C_DELIVERY, label="Delivery")
    supply_patch = mpatches.Patch(facecolor=_C_SUPPLY, label="Supply")
    fig.legend(handles=[delivery_patch, supply_patch], loc="upper center",
               ncol=2, fontsize=10, frameon=False, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, 0.97))
    return fig


def _tou_schedule_svg(display_name: str, tariff_code: str) -> None:
    """Emit a single SVG output so Quarto ``{{< embed >}}`` works (see quarto_manuscript_embed_bug.md)."""
    _fig = _make_tou_chart(display_name, tariff_code)
    display_svg(_fig)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-schedule-coned
#| fig-cap: "TOU rate schedule — ConEd"

_tou_schedule_svg("ConEd", "coned")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-schedule-nimo
#| fig-cap: "TOU rate schedule — National Grid"

_tou_schedule_svg("NiMo", "nimo")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-schedule-nyseg
#| fig-cap: "TOU rate schedule — NYSEG"

_tou_schedule_svg("NYSEG", "nyseg")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-schedule-cenhud
#| fig-cap: "TOU rate schedule — Central Hudson"

_tou_schedule_svg("CenHud", "cenhud")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-schedule-rge
#| fig-cap: "TOU rate schedule — RG&E"

_tou_schedule_svg("RG&E", "rge")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-schedule-or
#| fig-cap: "TOU rate schedule — O&R"

_tou_schedule_svg("O&R", "or")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-schedule-psegli
#| fig-cap: "TOU rate schedule — PSEG-LI"

_tou_schedule_svg("PSEG-LI", "psegli")
```

### Demand-flex: load shifting for a representative building

Under the TOU flex rate, customers have an incentive to shift electricity consumption from expensive peak hours to
cheaper off-peak hours. We model this response using a constant-elasticity demand model calibrated to empirical
evidence from the Arcturus 2.0 meta-analysis of TOU pricing pilots. The charts below illustrate this for a single
representative HP building in the `UTILITY` service territory: how much load shifts from peak to off-peak, and what
that means in dollar terms.

```{python}
#| label: tou-flex-shift-setup

if INCLUDE_TOU:
    _S3_FLEX_DIAG = "s3://data.sb/switchbox/demand_flex_calibration/ny/demand_flex_diagnostic.parquet"
    _flex_diag = pl.read_parquet(_S3_FLEX_DIAG)

    _flex_rec = (
        _flex_diag
        .filter(pl.col("utility") == UTILITY)
        .filter(pl.col("elasticity") == pl.col("seasonal_recommended_elasticity_with_tech"))
        .select("season", "seasonal_recommended_elasticity_with_tech", "price_ratio")
    )
    _flex_eps = {
        row["season"]: row["seasonal_recommended_elasticity_with_tech"]
        for row in _flex_rec.to_dicts()
    }

    _flex_meta = pl.read_parquet(
        "/ebs/data/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2_sb/metadata/state=NY/upgrade=00/metadata-sb.parquet"
    ).select("bldg_id", "postprocess_group.has_hp", "weight")
    _flex_util = pl.read_parquet(
        "/ebs/data/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2_sb/metadata_utility/state=NY/utility_assignment.parquet"
    ).select("bldg_id", "sb.electric_utility")
    _flex_hp = (
        _flex_meta.join(_flex_util, on="bldg_id", how="inner")
        .filter(
            (pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY)
            & (pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp") == True)  # noqa: E712
        )
    )

    _flex_bldg_id = int(_flex_hp["bldg_id"][0])

    _flex_hourly_path = Path(
        f"/ebs/data/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2_sb/load_curve_hourly/state={STATE}/upgrade=00/{_flex_bldg_id}-0.parquet"
    )
    _flex_hourly = pl.read_parquet(
        _flex_hourly_path,
        columns=["timestamp", "out.electricity.net.energy_consumption"],
    ).rename({"out.electricity.net.energy_consumption": "kwh"})

    _flex_tariff = _load_rate(f"{UTILITY}_{TARIFF_TOU}_supply_calibrated.json")
    _flex_rates_all = [tier[0]["rate"] for tier in _flex_tariff["energyratestructure"]]

    _WINTER_MONTHS = {1, 2, 3, 10, 11, 12}
    _SUMMER_MONTHS = {4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9}

    def _get_peak_hours_from_schedule(schedule: list[list[int]], month_idx: int) -> set[int]:
        row = schedule[month_idx]
        off_p = row[0]
        return {h for h, p in enumerate(row) if p != off_p}

    _winter_peak_hrs = _get_peak_hours_from_schedule(_flex_tariff["energyweekdayschedule"], 0)
    _summer_peak_hrs = _get_peak_hours_from_schedule(_flex_tariff["energyweekdayschedule"], 6)

    _season_cfg = {
        "winter": {
            "months": _WINTER_MONTHS, "peak_hours": _winter_peak_hrs,
            "off_rate": _flex_rates_all[0], "on_rate": _flex_rates_all[1],
            "epsilon": _flex_eps["winter"],
        },
        "summer": {
            "months": _SUMMER_MONTHS, "peak_hours": _summer_peak_hrs,
            "off_rate": _flex_rates_all[2], "on_rate": _flex_rates_all[3],
            "epsilon": _flex_eps["summer"],
        },
    }

    _flex_hourly = _flex_hourly.with_columns(
        pl.col("timestamp").dt.month().alias("month"),
        pl.col("timestamp").dt.hour().alias("hour"),
    )

    _flex_season_stats: dict[str, dict] = {}

    for _sn, _sc in _season_cfg.items():
        _sh = _flex_hourly.filter(pl.col("month").is_in(_sc["months"]))
        _is_peak = _sh["hour"].is_in(list(_sc["peak_hours"]))
        _peak_kwh = float(_sh.filter(_is_peak)["kwh"].sum())
        _offpeak_kwh = float(_sh.filter(~_is_peak)["kwh"].sum())
        _total_kwh = _peak_kwh + _offpeak_kwh
        _on_r = _sc["on_rate"]
        _off_r = _sc["off_rate"]
        _eps = _sc["epsilon"]

        _p_flat = (_peak_kwh * _on_r + _offpeak_kwh * _off_r) / _total_kwh
        _remaining_peak = _peak_kwh * (_on_r / _p_flat) ** _eps
        _shifted = _peak_kwh - _remaining_peak

        _flex_season_stats[_sn] = {
            "peak_kwh": _peak_kwh, "offpeak_kwh": _offpeak_kwh,
            "shifted_kwh": _shifted, "remaining_peak_kwh": _remaining_peak,
            "on_rate": _on_r, "off_rate": _off_r, "epsilon": _eps,
            "savings": _shifted * (_on_r - _off_r),
        }

    for _sn in ["winter", "summer"]:
        _fs = _flex_season_stats[_sn]
        _total = _fs["remaining_peak_kwh"] + _fs["shifted_kwh"] + _fs["offpeak_kwh"]
        _onpeak_total = _fs["remaining_peak_kwh"] + _fs["shifted_kwh"]
        report_vars[f"tou_flex_{_sn}_onpeak_share"] = _onpeak_total / _total
        report_vars[f"tou_flex_{_sn}_shift_share"] = _fs["shifted_kwh"] / _onpeak_total
        report_vars[f"tou_flex_{_sn}_savings"] = _fs["savings"]
        report_vars[f"tou_flex_{_sn}_on_off_ratio"] = _fs["on_rate"] / _fs["off_rate"]

    _FLEX_COLORS = {
        "Off-peak": SB_COLORS["sky"],
        "On-peak": SB_COLORS["saffron"],
        "Will shift": SB_COLORS["carrot"],
        "Shifted": SB_COLORS["midnight_text"],
    }

    _FLEX_BAR_HEIGHT = 0.35
    _FLEX_INTRA_GAP = 0.55
    _FLEX_GROUP_GAP = 0.6
    _FLEX_HEADER_SPACE = 0.45

    def _flex_bar_chart(
        groups: list[dict],
        colors: dict[str, str],
        value_fmt: str,
        fig_width: float = 12.5,
    ) -> "plt.Figure":
        """Horizontal stacked bar chart for demand-flex visualization.

        groups: list of dicts with "season" (str) and "bars" (list of dicts
        with "segments" list of (name, value) tuples, optional "label" str,
        optional "fmt" str to override value_fmt for that bar).
        value_fmt: "kwh" or "dollar" (default for all bars).
        """
        import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

        y = 0.0
        bar_specs: list[dict] = []
        season_headers: list[tuple[float, str]] = []

        for g_idx, g in enumerate(groups):
            season_headers.append((y, g["season"]))
            y += _FLEX_HEADER_SPACE

            for b_idx, bar in enumerate(g["bars"]):
                bar_specs.append({
                    "y": y, "segments": bar["segments"],
                    "label": bar.get("label", ""), "fmt": bar.get("fmt", value_fmt),
                })
                y += _FLEX_BAR_HEIGHT
                if b_idx < len(g["bars"]) - 1:
                    y += _FLEX_INTRA_GAP
            if g_idx < len(groups) - 1:
                y += _FLEX_GROUP_GAP

        y_max = y + 0.15
        fig_height = max(1.5, 0.4 + y_max * 0.9)

        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(fig_width, fig_height))
        ax.set_xlim(0, 105)
        ax.set_ylim(-0.1, y_max)
        ax.invert_yaxis()
        ax.set_xticks([])
        ax.set_yticks([])
        for spine in ax.spines.values():
            spine.set_visible(False)

        _MIN_INLINE = 8
        _max_by_fmt: dict[str, float] = {}
        for bs in bar_specs:
            fmt = bs["fmt"]
            t = sum(v for _, v in bs["segments"])
            if t > _max_by_fmt.get(fmt, 0):
                _max_by_fmt[fmt] = t

        for bs in bar_specs:
            total = sum(v for _, v in bs["segments"])
            if total == 0:
                continue
            scale = _max_by_fmt[bs["fmt"]]
            left = 0.0
            for seg_name, value in bs["segments"]:
                width = (value / scale) * 100
                ax.barh(bs["y"], width, left=left, height=_FLEX_BAR_HEIGHT,
                        color=colors[seg_name], edgecolor="none", align="edge")
                bar_fmt = bs["fmt"]
                val_str = f"{value:,.0f} kWh" if bar_fmt == "kwh" else f"${value:,.0f}"
                lbl = f"{val_str}\n({seg_name})"
                seg_mid_x = left + width / 2

                if width >= _MIN_INLINE:
                    ax.text(seg_mid_x, bs["y"] + _FLEX_BAR_HEIGHT / 2, lbl,
                            ha="center", va="center", color="white",
                            fontsize=9, fontweight="bold", fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans",
                            linespacing=1.1)
                elif value > 0:
                    offset_y = bs["y"] + _FLEX_BAR_HEIGHT + 0.12
                    ax.annotate(
                        lbl, xy=(seg_mid_x, bs["y"] + _FLEX_BAR_HEIGHT),
                        xytext=(seg_mid_x, offset_y),
                        ha="center", va="top", fontsize=8, fontweight="bold",
                        color=colors[seg_name], fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans",
                        linespacing=1.1,
                        arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="-", color=colors[seg_name], lw=0.8),
                    )
                left += width

            if bs["label"]:
                ax.text(left + 1, bs["y"] + _FLEX_BAR_HEIGHT / 2, bs["label"],
                        ha="left", va="center", fontsize=9, fontweight="bold",
                        fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans")

        for header_y, season_name in season_headers:
            ax.text(0, header_y, season_name,
                    ha="left", va="top", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold",
                    fontfamily="GT Planar", color="#333333")

        fig.subplots_adjust(left=0.02, right=0.90, top=0.92, bottom=0.02)
        return fig
```

We pick one HP building in the `UTILITY` territory and load its 8,760-hour load profile, assign each hour to
peak or off-peak based on the TOU flex tariff schedule, and compute the constant-elasticity demand shift:
$Q_\text{remaining} = Q_\text{peak} \times (P_\text{on} / P_\text{flat})^\varepsilon$.

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-flex-kwh-before
#| fig-cap: "Seasonal electricity consumption by TOU period for a representative HP building, showing the kWh that would shift under demand-flex"

if INCLUDE_TOU:
    _fig = _flex_bar_chart(
        groups=[
            {"season": f"{s.title()} (on:off = {_flex_season_stats[s]['on_rate'] / _flex_season_stats[s]['off_rate']:.1f}x)", "bars": [{"segments": [
                ("On-peak", _flex_season_stats[s]["remaining_peak_kwh"]),
                ("Will shift", _flex_season_stats[s]["shifted_kwh"]),
                ("Off-peak", _flex_season_stats[s]["offpeak_kwh"]),
            ]}]}
            for s in ["winter", "summer"]
        ],
        colors=_FLEX_COLORS,
        value_fmt="kwh",
    )
    display_svg(_fig)
```

Under the TOU flex rate with enabling technology, the demand-flex model predicts that this building would shift
a small fraction of its peak kWh to off-peak hours. The "Will shift" segment is the load that moves.
Energy is conserved — total seasonal kWh stays the same.

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-flex-kwh-shift
#| fig-cap: "Before and after demand-flex: seasonal kWh by TOU period for a representative HP building"

if INCLUDE_TOU:
    _fig = _flex_bar_chart(
        groups=[
            {"season": f"{s.title()} (on:off = {_flex_season_stats[s]['on_rate'] / _flex_season_stats[s]['off_rate']:.1f}x)", "bars": [
                {"segments": [
                    ("On-peak", _flex_season_stats[s]["remaining_peak_kwh"]),
                    ("Will shift", _flex_season_stats[s]["shifted_kwh"]),
                    ("Off-peak", _flex_season_stats[s]["offpeak_kwh"]),
                ], "label": "kWh before"},
                {"segments": [
                    ("On-peak", _flex_season_stats[s]["remaining_peak_kwh"]),
                    ("Shifted", _flex_season_stats[s]["shifted_kwh"]),
                    ("Off-peak", _flex_season_stats[s]["offpeak_kwh"]),
                ], "label": "kWh after"},
            ]}
            for s in ["winter", "summer"]
        ],
        colors=_FLEX_COLORS,
        value_fmt="kwh",
    )
    display_svg(_fig)
```

Both bars are the same width — total kWh is conserved. The "Will shift" chunk (before) becomes "Shifted"
(after), indicating the same kWh is now consumed during off-peak hours.

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-flex-bill-before
#| fig-cap: "Seasonal bill by TOU period for a representative HP building, showing the bill component that would change under demand-flex"

if INCLUDE_TOU:
    _s = _flex_season_stats
    _fig = _flex_bar_chart(
        groups=[
            {"season": f"{s.title()} (on:off = {_s[s]['on_rate'] / _s[s]['off_rate']:.1f}x)", "bars": [{"segments": [
                ("On-peak", _s[s]["remaining_peak_kwh"] * _s[s]["on_rate"]),
                ("Will shift", _s[s]["shifted_kwh"] * _s[s]["on_rate"]),
                ("Off-peak", _s[s]["offpeak_kwh"] * _s[s]["off_rate"]),
            ]}]}
            for s in ["winter", "summer"]
        ],
        colors=_FLEX_COLORS,
        value_fmt="dollar",
    )
    display_svg(_fig)
```

The "Will shift" segment here represents the dollar cost of the load that will move — priced at the expensive on-peak
rate. When this load moves to off-peak, it will be priced at the cheaper off-peak rate instead.

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-flex-bill-shift
#| fig-cap: "Before and after demand-flex: seasonal bill by TOU period for a representative HP building"

if INCLUDE_TOU:
    _s = _flex_season_stats
    _fig = _flex_bar_chart(
        groups=[
            {"season": f"{s.title()} (on:off = {_s[s]['on_rate'] / _s[s]['off_rate']:.1f}x)", "bars": [
                {"segments": [
                    ("On-peak", _s[s]["remaining_peak_kwh"] * _s[s]["on_rate"]),
                    ("Will shift", _s[s]["shifted_kwh"] * _s[s]["on_rate"]),
                    ("Off-peak", _s[s]["offpeak_kwh"] * _s[s]["off_rate"]),
                ], "label": "Bill before"},
                {"segments": [
                    ("On-peak", _s[s]["remaining_peak_kwh"] * _s[s]["on_rate"]),
                    ("Shifted", _s[s]["shifted_kwh"] * _s[s]["off_rate"]),
                    ("Off-peak", _s[s]["offpeak_kwh"] * _s[s]["off_rate"]),
                ], "label": "Bill after"},
            ]}
            for s in ["winter", "summer"]
        ],
        colors=_FLEX_COLORS,
        value_fmt="dollar",
    )
    display_svg(_fig)
```

The After bar is shorter than Before — the shifted kWh are now priced at the off-peak rate instead of the on-peak rate.
The width difference is the demand-flex savings.

Combining before/after for both kWh and bills into a single figure shows what moves and what it costs,
season by season.

```{python}
#| label: fig-tou-flex-combined-after
#| fig-cap: "Before and after demand-flex: seasonal kWh and bill for a representative HP building"

if INCLUDE_TOU:
    _s = _flex_season_stats
    _fig = _flex_bar_chart(
        groups=[
            {"season": f"{s.title()} (on:off = {_s[s]['on_rate'] / _s[s]['off_rate']:.1f}x)", "bars": [
                {"segments": [
                    ("On-peak", _s[s]["remaining_peak_kwh"]),
                    ("Will shift", _s[s]["shifted_kwh"]),
                    ("Off-peak", _s[s]["offpeak_kwh"]),
                ], "label": "kWh before", "fmt": "kwh"},
                {"segments": [
                    ("On-peak", _s[s]["remaining_peak_kwh"]),
                    ("Shifted", _s[s]["shifted_kwh"]),
                    ("Off-peak", _s[s]["offpeak_kwh"]),
                ], "label": "kWh after", "fmt": "kwh"},
                {"segments": [
                    ("On-peak", _s[s]["remaining_peak_kwh"] * _s[s]["on_rate"]),
                    ("Will shift", _s[s]["shifted_kwh"] * _s[s]["on_rate"]),
                    ("Off-peak", _s[s]["offpeak_kwh"] * _s[s]["off_rate"]),
                ], "label": "Bill before", "fmt": "dollar"},
                {"segments": [
                    ("On-peak", _s[s]["remaining_peak_kwh"] * _s[s]["on_rate"]),
                    ("Shifted", _s[s]["shifted_kwh"] * _s[s]["off_rate"]),
                    ("Off-peak", _s[s]["offpeak_kwh"] * _s[s]["off_rate"]),
                ], "label": "Bill after", "fmt": "dollar"},
            ]}
            for s in ["winter", "summer"]
        ],
        colors=_FLEX_COLORS,
        value_fmt="kwh",
    )
    display_svg(_fig)
```

## Cross-subsidization under fair rate

Does the seasonal rate actually fix the cross-subsidy? We compare the BAT
distributions under flat rates (run 1+2) and HP seasonal rates (`RUN_HPRATE`) — same
buildings, same upgrade 0, only the rate structure changes. If the seasonal rate
is well-designed, the HP overpayment should collapse to near zero.

```{python}
#| label: bat-56-ingest

bat_hprate = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BAT_HPRATE, hive_partitioning=True)

bat_all_56 = (
    bat_hprate
    .select(
        BLDG_ID, "weight",
        BAT_DELIVERY,
        BAT_SUPPLY,
        HEATING_TYPE_COL,
        "sb.electric_utility",
    )
    .with_columns((pl.col(BAT_DELIVERY) + pl.col(BAT_SUPPLY)).alias(BAT_TOTAL))
    .collect()
)
```

### Summary: HP overpayment before vs. after

The comparison table shows how the mean delivery BAT changes for each heating
type when HP customers move from the default flat rate to the seasonal rate.

```{python}
#| label: bat-comparison-table

bat_hprate_for_comp = (
    bat_hprate
    .select(BLDG_ID, "weight", BAT_DELIVERY, HEATING_TYPE_COL,
            "heats_with_natgas", "heats_with_oil", "heats_with_propane")
    .collect()
)

_comp_rows: list[dict] = []
for label, filt in [
    ("Heat pump", pl.col("heating_label") == "Heat pump"),
    ("Natural gas", pl.col("heats_with_natgas")),
    ("Oil / propane", pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane")),
    ("Electric resistance", pl.col("heating_label") == "Electric resistance"),
]:
    sub_before = labeled_all.filter(filt)
    sub_after = bat_hprate_for_comp.with_columns(
        pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL)
        .replace_strict(HEATING_TYPE_LABELS, default=pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL))
        .fill_null("Other")
        .alias("heating_label"),
    ).filter(filt)
    _comp_rows.append({
        "heating_type": label,
        "current": _weighted_mean(sub_before, BAT_DELIVERY, "weight"),
        "seasonal": _weighted_mean(sub_after, BAT_DELIVERY, "weight"),
    })

```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-bat-comparison

_bat_comp_gt = (
    GT(pl.DataFrame(_comp_rows), rowname_col="heating_type")
    .fmt_currency(columns=["current", "seasonal"], decimals=0)
    .cols_label(
        current="Current flat rates",
        seasonal="HP seasonal rate",
    )
    .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options())
)
display_gt(_bat_comp_gt)
```

## Bill impact of fair rate on HP customers

What happens to a fossil-fuel customer's total energy bill if they switch to a
heat pump **and** get the proposed HP seasonal delivery rate? The "before" is
their current bill under today's flat rates (run 1+2, upgrade 0) and the
"after" is their bill as an HP customer on the seasonal rate (`RUN_HPRATE_HP`,
upgrade 2). The delta captures both the fuel switch and the rate change.

```{python}
#| label: compute-deltas-switch-with-hprate

annual_before_switch_state = (
    bills_current
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(DELTA_SELECT)
    .collect()
)
annual_after_switch_state = (
    bills_hprate_hp
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after"))
    .collect()
)
delta_switch_state = annual_before_switch_state.join(annual_after_switch_state, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
    (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
)
```

### Statewide bill changes by fuel type (fair rate)

```{python}
#| label: compute-switch-deltas-by-fuel

natgas_switch_state = (
    delta_switch_state
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)

_sw_total_w = natgas_switch_state["weight"].sum()
_sw_save_w = natgas_switch_state.filter(pl.col("delta") < 0)["weight"].sum()
_sw_lose_1k_w = natgas_switch_state.filter(pl.col("delta") > 1000)["weight"].sum()
_sw_save_1k_w = natgas_switch_state.filter(pl.col("delta") < -1000)["weight"].sum()
report_vars["pct_natgas_save_hprate"] = _sw_save_w / _sw_total_w
report_vars["pct_natgas_lose_1k_hprate"] = _sw_lose_1k_w / _sw_total_w
report_vars["pct_natgas_save_1k_hprate"] = _sw_save_1k_w / _sw_total_w
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-natgas-default-vs-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change comparison — natural gas homes switching to HP: default vs. seasonal rate (Statewide)"

_comparison_rows = [("Default rate", natgas_state), ("HP seasonal rate", natgas_switch_state)]

_all_deltas = pl.concat([r[1] for r in _comparison_rows])
_snap = max(BIN_WIDTH, 50)
_x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(_all_deltas, "delta", "weight", 0.01) / _snap) * _snap
_x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(_all_deltas, "delta", "weight", 0.99) / _snap) * _snap

_parts = []
_label_records: list[dict] = []
for _label, _df in _comparison_rows:
    _binned = (
        _df.with_columns(
            ((pl.col("delta") / BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BIN_WIDTH + BIN_WIDTH / 2).alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") <= -1000).then(pl.lit("savings > $1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") > -1000) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 0)).then(pl.lit("savings $0-1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") >= 0) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 1000)).then(pl.lit("losses $0-1k"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("losses > $1k"))
            .alias("quadrant"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "quadrant")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
        .with_columns(pl.lit(_label).alias("rate"))
    )
    _parts.append(_binned)

_global_max_w = max(
    float(p["weight_sum"].max()) for p in _parts if not p.is_empty()
)
_y_annot = _global_max_w * 1.05

for _label, _df in _comparison_rows:
    _pct = quadrant_pcts(_df)
    _q_bounds = [(_x_lo, -1000), (-1000, 0), (0, 1000), (1000, _x_hi)]
    _label_xs = [(max(lo, _x_lo) + min(hi, _x_hi)) / 2 for lo, hi in _q_bounds]
    for _q, _xp in zip(QUADRANT_ORDER, _label_xs):
        if _pct[_q] < 0.05:
            continue
        _label_records.append({
            "rate": _label, "x": _xp, "y": _y_annot,
            "text": f"{_q}\n{_pct[_q]:.1f}%", "color": QUADRANT_COLORS[_q],
        })

_rate_dtype = pl.Enum([r[0] for r in _comparison_rows])
_plot_df = pl.concat(_parts).with_columns(
    pl.col("quadrant").cast(pl.Enum(QUADRANT_ORDER)),
    pl.col("rate").cast(_rate_dtype),
)
_label_df = pl.DataFrame(_label_records).with_columns(pl.col("rate").cast(_rate_dtype))

(
    ggplot(_plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="quadrant"))
    + geom_col(width=BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
    + geom_vline(xintercept=[-1000, 0, 1000], linetype="dotted", color="gray")
    + geom_text(
        _label_df, aes(x="x", y="y", label="text", color="color"),
        inherit_aes=False, ha="center", size=9,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=QUADRANT_COLORS, breaks=QUADRANT_ORDER)
    + scale_color_identity()
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(_x_lo, _x_hi))
    + facet_wrap("rate", ncol=1)
    + labs(x="Annual Bill Change ($)", y="# of Homes (weighted)")
    + guides(fill=False)
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(10.5, 6),
        strip_text=element_text(ha="left"),
    )
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-oil-propane-switch-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — oil/propane homes switching to HP with seasonal rate (Statewide)"

oil_propane_switch_state = (
    delta_switch_state
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)

_op_sw_total = oil_propane_switch_state["weight"].sum()
_op_sw_save = oil_propane_switch_state.filter(pl.col("delta") < 0)["weight"].sum()
_op_sw_lose_1k = oil_propane_switch_state.filter(pl.col("delta") > 1000)["weight"].sum()
report_vars["pct_oil_propane_save_hprate"] = _op_sw_save / _op_sw_total
report_vars["pct_oil_propane_lose_1k_hprate"] = _op_sw_lose_1k / _op_sw_total
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-elec-resistance-switch-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — electric resistance homes switching to HP with seasonal rate (Statewide)"

elec_resistance_switch_state = (
    delta_switch_state
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")
    .select("delta", "weight")
)

_er_sw_total = elec_resistance_switch_state["weight"].sum()
_er_sw_save = elec_resistance_switch_state.filter(pl.col("delta") < 0)["weight"].sum()
_er_sw_lose_1k = elec_resistance_switch_state.filter(pl.col("delta") > 1000)["weight"].sum()
report_vars["pct_elec_resistance_save_hprate"] = _er_sw_save / _er_sw_total
report_vars["pct_elec_resistance_lose_1k_hprate"] = _er_sw_lose_1k / _er_sw_total
```

### Representative home bill decomposition: default vs. fair rates

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-decomposed-fair-rate-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual energy bill for the representative gas-heated Buffalo home: before heat pumps, after heat pumps at default rates, and after heat pumps at fair rates."

import io

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import numpy as np

_SAFFRON = "#ffc729"
_scenarios = ["Natural gas\nfurnace", "Heat pump\n(default rates)", "Heat pump\n(fair rates)"]
_x = np.arange(len(_scenarios))
_w = 0.55

_bars_data = []
for _med in [median_current_state, median_hp_state, median_hp_hprate_state]:
    _bars_data.append({
        "fixed": float(_med["delivery_fixed"][0]),
        "vol": float(_med["delivery_volumetric"][0]),
        "supply": float(_med["supply"][0]),
        "gas": float(_med["gas"][0]),
    })

_comp_keys = ["fixed", "vol", "supply", "gas"]
_comp_colors = {
    "fixed": DECOMPOSED_COLORS["Electric Delivery Bill (Fixed)"],
    "vol": DECOMPOSED_COLORS["Electric Delivery Bill (Volumetric)"],
    "supply": DECOMPOSED_COLORS["Electric Supply Bill"],
    "gas": DECOMPOSED_COLORS["Gas Bill"],
}
_comp_label_colors = {
    "fixed": "#023047",
    "vol": "#56B4E9",
    "supply": "#E69F00",
    "gas": "#888888",
}

fig_3bar, ax_3bar = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 7))

_bottoms = [0.0] * 3
for _ck in _comp_keys:
    _vals = [_bars_data[i][_ck] for i in range(3)]
    ax_3bar.bar(_x, _vals, _w, bottom=_bottoms, color=_comp_colors[_ck], edgecolor="none")
    for _i, (_v, _b) in enumerate(zip(_vals, _bottoms)):
        if _v > 80:
            ax_3bar.text(_x[_i], _b + _v / 2, _format_dollar(_v), ha="center", va="center",
                         color="white", fontweight="bold", fontsize=11, zorder=11)
    _bottoms = [_b + _v for _b, _v in zip(_bottoms, _vals)]

_totals = [sum(_bars_data[i][k] for k in _comp_keys) for i in range(3)]

for _i in (0, 1):
    ax_3bar.text(_x[_i], _totals[_i] + 50, _format_dollar(_totals[_i]),
                 ha="center", va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold", fontsize=12)

_delivery_before = _bars_data[0]["fixed"] + _bars_data[0]["vol"]
ax_3bar.hlines(
    y=_delivery_before, xmin=-0.5, xmax=_x[2] + _w / 2,
    color=_SAFFRON, linewidth=1.8, linestyle=(0, (6, 4)), zorder=10,
)

_savings = _totals[1] - _totals[2]
_pct_savings = _savings / _totals[1] * 100

_rect = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch(
    (_x[2] - _w / 2, _totals[2]), _w, _savings,
    boxstyle="square,pad=0",
    facecolor="#FFC729", alpha=0.12,
    edgecolor="#B8960A", linewidth=2.0, linestyle=(0, (5, 3)),
    hatch="////", zorder=3,
)
ax_3bar.add_patch(_rect)

ax_3bar.text(_x[2], _totals[2] + 50, _format_dollar(_totals[2]),
             ha="center", va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold", fontsize=12,
             zorder=8)

ax_3bar.annotate(
    "", xy=(_x[2], _totals[2] + 180), xytext=(_x[2], _totals[1] - 60),
    arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->,head_length=0.4,head_width=0.25", color=_SAFFRON, lw=2.2),
    zorder=6,
)

ax_3bar.text(_x[2], _totals[1] + 180, f"-{_pct_savings:.0f}%",
             ha="center", va="bottom", color=_SAFFRON, fontweight="bold", fontsize=14)
ax_3bar.text(_x[2], _totals[1] + 50, f"-{_format_dollar(_savings)}",
             ha="center", va="bottom", color=_SAFFRON, fontsize=11, alpha=0.8)

_side_x = _x[2] + _w / 2 + 0.15
_d3 = _bars_data[2]
_side_specs = [
    ("Delivery\n(Fixed)", _d3["fixed"] / 2, _comp_label_colors["fixed"]),
    ("Delivery\n(Volumetric)", _d3["fixed"] + _d3["vol"] / 2, _comp_label_colors["vol"]),
    ("Supply", _d3["fixed"] + _d3["vol"] + _d3["supply"] / 2, _comp_label_colors["supply"]),
    ("Gas", _d3["fixed"] + _d3["vol"] + _d3["supply"] + _d3["gas"] / 2, _comp_label_colors["gas"]),
]
for _lbl, _ym, _clr in _side_specs:
    ax_3bar.plot([_x[2] + _w / 2 + 0.02, _side_x - 0.03], [_ym, _ym],
                 color=_clr, linewidth=0.7, alpha=0.6)
    ax_3bar.text(_side_x, _ym, _lbl, color=_clr, ha="left", va="center",
                 fontsize=8.5, fontweight="bold")

ax_3bar.set_xticks(_x)
ax_3bar.set_xticklabels(_scenarios, fontsize=11)
ax_3bar.set_ylabel("Annual energy bill ($)", fontsize=12)
ax_3bar.set_ylim(0, max(_totals) * 1.22)
ax_3bar.set_xlim(-0.5, _x[2] + _w / 2 + 0.7)
ax_3bar.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax_3bar.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
ax_3bar.spines["bottom"].set_bounds(-0.5, _x[2] + _w / 2)

report_vars["fair_rate_savings_dollar"] = _savings
report_vars["fair_rate_savings_pct"] = _pct_savings / 100
report_vars["fair_rate_total_bill"] = _totals[2]

fig_3bar.tight_layout()
display_svg(fig_3bar)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-natgas-switch-hprate-by-utility
#| fig-cap: "Change in total annual energy bills — natural gas homes switching to HP: default vs. seasonal rate, by utility"

plot_quadrant_bar_by_utility(
    delta_state, delta_switch_state,
    utility_names=UTIL_NAMES,
    fuel_filter=pl.col("heats_with_natgas"),
    title_parts=[
        ("How bills would change for ", "#000000"),
        ("natural gas", "#7A8A10"),
        (" heated homes after switching to ", "#000000"),
        ("heat pumps", "#c47600"),
    ],
)
```

### Adding TOU to the comparison

What happens if HP customers get the TOU flex rate instead of the flat seasonal rate? The
"before" is their current bill under today's flat rates (run 1+2, upgrade 0) and the "after" is their bill as an HP
customer on the calibrated TOU flex rate (`RUN_TOU_HP`, upgrade 2).

```{python}
#| label: compute-deltas-tou

if INCLUDE_TOU:
    annual_after_tou_state = (
        bills_tou_hp
        .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after"))
        .collect()
    )
    delta_tou_state = annual_current_state.join(annual_after_tou_state, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
        (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
    )
    natgas_tou_state = delta_tou_state.filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas")).select("delta", "weight")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-natgas-tou-state
#| fig-cap: "Change in total annual energy bills — natural gas homes switching to HP: default vs. fair vs. TOU + fair rate (Statewide)"

if INCLUDE_TOU:
    _tou_quadrant_fig = plot_quadrant_bar(
        [
            ("Default rate", natgas_state),
            ("Fair rate", natgas_switch_state),
            ("TOU flex + fair rate", natgas_tou_state),
        ],
        title_parts=[
            ("How bills would change for ", "#000000"),
            ("natural gas", "#7A8A10"),
            (" heated homes after switching to ", "#000000"),
            ("heat pumps", "#c47600"),
        ],
    )
    _q_fair = quadrant_pcts(natgas_switch_state)
    _q_tou = quadrant_pcts(natgas_tou_state)
    _fair_lose = _q_fair["losses $0-1k"] + _q_fair["losses > $1k"]
    _tou_lose = _q_tou["losses $0-1k"] + _q_tou["losses > $1k"]
    report_vars["tou_lose_drop_pp"] = (_fair_lose - _tou_lose) / 100
    report_vars["tou_save_1k_rise_pp"] = (_q_tou["savings > $1k"] - _q_fair["savings > $1k"]) / 100

    def _weighted_mean_savings(df: pl.DataFrame) -> float:
        savers = df.filter(pl.col("delta") < 0)
        return float((-savers["delta"] * savers["weight"]).sum() / savers["weight"].sum())

    report_vars["tou_avg_savings_rise"] = _weighted_mean_savings(natgas_tou_state) - _weighted_mean_savings(natgas_switch_state)
else:
    _tou_quadrant_fig = None
_tou_quadrant_fig
```

The quadrant picture barely moves between the fair rate and the TOU variant. To see why, we compare each
building's annual bill under TOU flex (`RUN_TOU_HP`) vs. seasonal (`RUN_HPRATE_HP`) directly — the difference isolates the
rate-design effect without the fuel-switching effect.

```{python}
#| label: compute-tou-vs-seasonal-delta

if INCLUDE_TOU:
    _annual_seasonal_hp = (
        bills_hprate_hp
        .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_seasonal"),
                "heats_with_natgas", "weight")
        .collect()
    )
    _annual_tou_hp = (
        bills_tou_hp
        .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_tou"))
        .collect()
    )
    _tou_vs_seasonal = (
        _annual_seasonal_hp
        .join(_annual_tou_hp, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner")
        .with_columns((pl.col("bill_tou") - pl.col("bill_seasonal")).alias("diff_energy_total"))
    )

    _natgas_w = _tou_vs_seasonal.filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))

    _total_w = _natgas_w["weight"].sum()
    _within_50 = _natgas_w.filter(pl.col("diff_energy_total").abs() <= 50)["weight"].sum()
    report_vars["pct_within_50"] = _within_50 / _total_w
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-histogram-tou-delta-weighted
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of annual bill change when switching from the seasonal HP rate to the TOU HP rate (natural gas homes)"

if INCLUDE_TOU:
    _BW = 10
    _binned = (
        _natgas_w
        .with_columns(
            ((pl.col("diff_energy_total") / _BW).floor() * _BW + _BW / 2).alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weighted_count"))
        .filter((pl.col("bin_center") >= -200) & (pl.col("bin_center") <= 200))
        .sort("bin_center")
    )

    _tou_hist_fig = (
        ggplot(_binned, aes(x="bin_center", y="weighted_count"))
        + geom_col(width=_BW * 0.9, fill=SB_COLORS["sky"])
        + geom_vline(xintercept=0, color="red", linetype="dashed", size=0.8)
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(-200, 200))
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.05, 0))
        + labs(
            title="Annual bill change, TOU − seasonal HP rate",
            x="Annual bill change ($/yr)",
            y="Number of homes",
        )
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(figure_size=(10.5, 4.5))
    )
else:
    _tou_hist_fig = None
_tou_hist_fig
```

### LMI scenarios — default vs. fair rate (natgas, statewide)

We recompute the two-bar comparison (default rate vs. seasonal rate) using LMI-discounted bill columns. The
seasonal-rate "after" comes from `bills_hprate_hp` (`RUN_HPRATE_HP`) instead of `bills_hp` (runs 3+4).

```{python}
#| label: compute-lmi-switch-deltas-state

delta_switch_state_lmi40 = _lmi_delta_state(
    bills_current, bills_hprate_hp, LMI_40_BILL_COLS, LMI_40_BILL_COLS,
)
delta_switch_state_lmi_full = _lmi_delta_state(
    bills_current, bills_hprate_hp, LMI_40_BILL_COLS, LMI_100_BILL_COLS,
)

natgas_switch_state_lmi40 = (
    delta_switch_state_lmi40
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
_lmi40_total = natgas_switch_state_lmi40["weight"].sum()
_lmi40_save = natgas_switch_state_lmi40.filter(pl.col("delta") < 0)["weight"].sum()
_lmi40_lose_1k = natgas_switch_state_lmi40.filter(pl.col("delta") > 1000)["weight"].sum()
report_vars["pct_natgas_save_hprate_lmi40"] = _lmi40_save / _lmi40_total
report_vars["pct_natgas_lose_1k_hprate_lmi40"] = _lmi40_lose_1k / _lmi40_total

natgas_switch_state_lmi_full = (
    delta_switch_state_lmi_full
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
_lmif_total = natgas_switch_state_lmi_full["weight"].sum()
_lmif_save = natgas_switch_state_lmi_full.filter(pl.col("delta") < 0)["weight"].sum()
_lmif_save_1k = natgas_switch_state_lmi_full.filter(pl.col("delta") < -1000)["weight"].sum()
report_vars["pct_natgas_save_hprate_lmi_full"] = _lmif_save / _lmif_total
report_vars["pct_natgas_save_1k_hprate_lmi_full"] = _lmif_save_1k / _lmif_total
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-natgas-switch-hprate-state-lmi-current
#| fig-cap: "Change in total annual energy bills — natural gas homes switching to HP: default vs. seasonal rate (Statewide)"

plot_quadrant_bar(
    [
        ("Default rate", natgas_state_lmi40),
        ("HP seasonal rate", natgas_switch_state_lmi40),
    ],
    title_parts=[
        ("How bills would change for ", "#000000"),
        ("natural gas", "#7A8A10"),
        (" heated homes after switching to ", "#000000"),
        ("heat pumps", "#c47600"),
    ],
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-oil-propane-switch-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Change in total annual energy bills — oil/propane homes switching to HP: default vs. seasonal rate (Statewide)"

plot_quadrant_bar(
    [
        ("Default rate", oil_propane_state),
        ("HP seasonal rate", oil_propane_switch_state),
    ],
    title_parts=[
        ("How bills would change for ", "#000000"),
        ("fuel oil", "#4a8fa0"),
        (" or ", "#000000"),
        ("propane", "#3a8fa0"),
        (" heated homes after switching to ", "#000000"),
        ("heat pumps", "#c47600"),
    ],
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-oil-propane-switch-hprate-by-utility
#| fig-cap: "Change in total annual energy bills — oil/propane homes switching to HP: default vs. seasonal rate, by utility"

plot_quadrant_bar_by_utility(
    delta_state, delta_switch_state,
    utility_names=UTIL_NAMES,
    fuel_filter=pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"),
    title_parts=[
        ("How bills would change for ", "#000000"),
        ("fuel oil", "#4a8fa0"),
        (" or ", "#000000"),
        ("propane", "#3a8fa0"),
        (" heated homes after switching to ", "#000000"),
        ("heat pumps", "#c47600"),
    ],
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-elec-resistance-switch-hprate-state
#| fig-cap: "Change in total annual energy bills — electric resistance homes switching to HP: default vs. seasonal rate (Statewide)"

plot_quadrant_bar(
    [
```
