```{python}
        ("Default rate", elec_resistance_state),
        ("HP seasonal rate", elec_resistance_switch_state),
    ],
    title_parts=[
        ("How bills would change for ", "#000000"),
        ("electric resistance", "#b08e00"),
        (" heated homes after switching to ", "#000000"),
        ("heat pumps", "#c47600"),
    ],
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-elec-resistance-switch-hprate-by-utility
#| fig-cap: "Change in total annual energy bills — electric resistance homes switching to HP: default vs. seasonal rate, by utility"

plot_quadrant_bar_by_utility(
    delta_state, delta_switch_state,
    utility_names=UTIL_NAMES,
    fuel_filter=pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance",
    title_parts=[
        ("How bills would change for ", "#000000"),
        ("electric resistance", "#b08e00"),
        (" heated homes after switching to ", "#000000"),
        ("heat pumps", "#c47600"),
    ],
)
```

## Bill impact of HP seasonal rate on non-HP customers

What happens to fossil-fuel customers' bills if all existing HP customers opt in to the seasonal rate — but the non-HP
customers themselves stay on their current heating system (upgrade 0) and on the recalibrated flat rate?

In the HP seasonal precalc run (`RUN_HPRATE`), the HP seasonal rate is applied to HP customers and the flat rate is recalibrated for everyone else. Non-HP
customers keep the same heating system; what changes is a slight adjustment to the flat rate once the HP cross-subsidy
is removed. Comparing run 1+2 vs `RUN_HPRATE` for fossil-fuel buildings shows this (expected to be very small) impact.

```{python}
#| label: compute-deltas-nonhp-rebal

annual_before_rebal_state = (
    bills_current
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(DELTA_SELECT)
    .collect()
)
annual_after_rebal_state = (
    bills_hprate_current
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after"))
    .collect()
)
delta_rebal_state = annual_before_rebal_state.join(annual_after_rebal_state, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
    (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
)
```

### Statewide — non-HP bill impact of cross-subsidy removal

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-non-hp-rebal-state
#| fig-cap: "Average monthly bill change — all non-HP homes after cross-subsidy removal (Statewide)"

REBAL_3_COLORS = {
    "bill decrease": "#81c784",
    "increase < $5/mo": "#ef9a9a",
    "increase ≥ $5/mo": "#b71c1c",
}
REBAL_3_ORDER = list(REBAL_3_COLORS.keys())
REBAL_3_LABELS = {
    "bill decrease": "DECREASE",
    "increase < $5/mo": "< $5/MO",
    "increase ≥ $5/mo": "≥ $5/MO",
}

monthly_rebal = (
    delta_rebal_state
    .filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp") == False)
    .with_columns((pl.col("delta") / 12).alias("delta_monthly"))
)

total_w = monthly_rebal["weight"].sum()
_pct_rebal = {
    "bill decrease": monthly_rebal.filter(pl.col("delta_monthly") < 0)["weight"].sum() / total_w * 100,
    "increase < $5/mo": monthly_rebal.filter((pl.col("delta_monthly") >= 0) & (pl.col("delta_monthly") < 5))["weight"].sum() / total_w * 100,
    "increase ≥ $5/mo": monthly_rebal.filter(pl.col("delta_monthly") >= 5)["weight"].sum() / total_w * 100,
}

_records = [{"scenario": "All non-HP", "category": k, "pct": v} for k, v in _pct_rebal.items()]
_plot_df = pl.DataFrame(_records).with_columns(
    pl.col("category").cast(pl.Enum(REBAL_3_ORDER)),
    pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(["All non-HP"])),
)

_stacking_order = list(reversed(REBAL_3_ORDER))
_annotations: list = []
_cum = 0.0
for _q in _stacking_order:
    _mid = _cum + _pct_rebal[_q] / 2
    _cum += _pct_rebal[_q]
    if _pct_rebal[_q] < 2:
        continue
    _annotations.append(
        annotate(
            "text",
            x=1.45,
            y=_mid,
            label=REBAL_3_LABELS[_q],
            ha="center", va="bottom",
            size=8, color=REBAL_3_COLORS[_q], fontweight="bold",
        )
    )

p = (
    ggplot(_plot_df, aes(x="scenario", y="pct", fill="category"))
    + geom_col(position="stack", width=0.55)
    + geom_text(
        _plot_df.filter(pl.col("pct") >= 3),
        aes(label="pct"),
        position=position_stack(vjust=0.5),
        format_string="{:.0f}%",
        color="white",
        size=10,
        fontweight="bold",
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=REBAL_3_COLORS, breaks=REBAL_3_ORDER)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.15, 0))
    + scale_x_discrete(expand=(0, 0.35, 0, 1.2))
    + coord_flip()
    + guides(fill=False)
    + labs(x="", y="", title="Monthly bill change for non-HP homes after cross-subsidy removal")
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(12.5, 2.6),
        axis_text=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_x=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks_y=element_blank(),
        axis_line_x=element_blank(),
        axis_line_y=element_blank(),
        panel_grid=element_blank(),
        plot_title=element_text(ha="left", margin={"b": -8, "units": "pt"}),
    )
)
for _a in _annotations:
    p = p + _a
p
```

```{python}
#| label: rebal-report-vars

report_vars["rebal_nonhp_mean_monthly_delta"] = _weighted_mean(
    monthly_rebal, "delta_monthly", "weight"
)
report_vars["rebal_nonhp_median_monthly_delta"] = _weighted_percentile(
    monthly_rebal, "delta_monthly", "weight", 0.50
)
report_vars["rebal_nonhp_pct_decrease"] = _pct_rebal["bill decrease"] / 100
report_vars["rebal_nonhp_pct_increase_under_5"] = _pct_rebal["increase < $5/mo"] / 100
report_vars["rebal_nonhp_pct_increase_over_5"] = _pct_rebal["increase ≥ $5/mo"] / 100
```

## Energy burden for low-income gas-heated households

What share of low-income, gas-heated households face a high energy burden — defined as spending more than 6% of annual
income on energy — and how does that share change when they switch to heat pumps under different rate designs?

We focus on low-and-moderate-income (LMI) households — identified by the `is_lmi` flag in the master bill tables —
who heat with natural gas in the baseline scenario (upgrade 0). We track the same cohort of buildings across three
scenarios:

1. **Current HVAC, default rates** (runs 1+2) — the status quo
2. **Heat pump, default rates** (runs 3+4) — electrified but on flat rates
3. **Heat pump, HP seasonal rates** (`RUN_HPRATE_HP`) — electrified on the proposed seasonal rate

```{python}
#| label: burden-cohort

low_income_gas_bldgs = (
    bills_current
    .filter(
        (pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        & pl.col("heats_with_natgas")
        & pl.col("is_lmi_any")
        & (pl.col(INCOME_COL) > 0)
    )
    .select(BLDG_ID, "weight", "energy_total_bill", INCOME_COL, "sb.electric_utility")
    .collect()
)

cohort_bldg_ids = low_income_gas_bldgs[BLDG_ID].to_list()
n_cohort = len(cohort_bldg_ids)
n_cohort_weighted = low_income_gas_bldgs["weight"].sum()
```

```{python}
#| label: burden-compute

def burden_shares(
    bills_lf: pl.LazyFrame,
    bldg_ids: list,
    income_df: pl.DataFrame,
    bill_cols: list[str] = LMI_BILL_COLS,
    burden_threshold: float = BURDEN_THRESHOLD,
) -> tuple[float, float]:
    """Return (weighted_pct_below, weighted_pct_above) the burden threshold.

    bill_cols controls which columns are summed to form the total energy bill.
    Default uses the undiscounted columns; pass LMI_40_BILL_COLS or
    LMI_100_BILL_COLS to use LMI-discounted bills.
    """
    annual = (
        bills_lf
        .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .select(BLDG_ID, "weight", *bill_cols)
        .collect()
        .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(bldg_ids))
        .with_columns(
            pl.sum_horizontal(bill_cols).alias("total_bill"),
        )
        .join(income_df.select(BLDG_ID, INCOME_COL), on=BLDG_ID, how="inner")
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("total_bill") / pl.col(INCOME_COL)).alias("burden"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("burden") > burden_threshold).alias("is_burdened"),
        )
    )
    total_weight = annual["weight"].sum()
    burdened_weight = annual.filter(pl.col("is_burdened"))["weight"].sum()
    pct_above = burdened_weight / total_weight * 100
    pct_below = 100 - pct_above
    return (pct_below, pct_above)

income_df = low_income_gas_bldgs.select(BLDG_ID, INCOME_COL)

below_current, above_current = burden_shares(bills_current, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df)
below_hp_default, above_hp_default = burden_shares(bills_hp, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df)
below_hp_seasonal, above_hp_seasonal = burden_shares(bills_hprate_hp, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df)

print(f"Current HVAC:        {above_current:.1f}% above 6% | {below_current:.1f}% below")
print(f"HP + default rates:  {above_hp_default:.1f}% above 6% | {below_hp_default:.1f}% below")
print(f"HP + seasonal rates: {above_hp_seasonal:.1f}% above 6% | {below_hp_seasonal:.1f}% below")
```

```{python}
#| label: burden-bar-helper

BURDEN_SCENARIO_LABELS = [
    f"Current HVAC\nDefault Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    f"Heat Pump\nDefault Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    f"Heat Pump\nHP Seasonal Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    "Heat Pump\nHP Seasonal Rates\n100% LMI Discounts",
]
BURDEN_SEGMENT_ORDER = ["Above 6%", "Below 6%"]
BURDEN_COLORS = {
    "Above 6%": SB_COLORS["carrot"],
    "Below 6%": SB_COLORS["sky"],
}


def plot_burden_bar(
    below_pcts: list[float],
    above_pcts: list[float],
    scenario_labels: list[str] = BURDEN_SCENARIO_LABELS,
):
    """Diverging bar chart of burden shares centered on the 6% threshold.

    Orange bars (above 6%) extend upward from y=0; blue bars (below 6%)
    extend downward. The y=0 line represents the burden threshold.
    """
    rows = []
    for scenario, above, below in zip(scenario_labels, above_pcts, below_pcts):
        rows.append({"scenario": scenario, "segment": "Above 6%",
                     "value": above, "label": f"{round(above)}%",
                     "label_y": above / 2})
        rows.append({"scenario": scenario, "segment": "Below 6%",
                     "value": -below, "label": f"{round(below)}%",
                     "label_y": -below / 2})

    df = pl.DataFrame(rows).with_columns(
        pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(scenario_labels)),
        pl.col("segment").cast(pl.Enum(BURDEN_SEGMENT_ORDER)),
    )

    return (
        ggplot(df, aes(x="scenario", y="value", fill="segment"))
        + geom_col(width=0.5)
        + geom_text(
            aes(y="label_y", label="label"),
            color="white", fontweight="bold", size=11,
        )
        + geom_hline(yintercept=0, size=1.5, color="#333333")
        + annotate(
            "text",
            x=0.45, y=0,
            label="6% Burden\nThreshold",
            ha="right", va="center",
            fontweight="bold", size=11, color="#333333", family="IBM Plex Sans",
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=BURDEN_COLORS)
        + scale_x_discrete(limits=scenario_labels, expand=(0, 0.8, 0, 0.5))
        + scale_y_continuous(
            expand=(0.05, 0, 0.05, 0),
            labels=lambda xs: ["" for _ in xs],
        )
        + guides(fill=False)
        + labs(x="", y="")
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(
            figure_size=(10, 7),
            axis_text_y=element_blank(),
            axis_ticks_major_y=element_blank(),
            axis_ticks_minor_y=element_blank(),
            axis_line_y=element_blank(),
            panel_grid=element_blank(),
            axis_line_x=element_blank(),
            axis_ticks_major_x=element_blank(),
        )
    )
```

### With current LMI discount enrollment (~40%)

An estimated 40% of LMI households currently receive LMI discounts on their utility bills. We recompute energy burdens
using the `_lmi_40` bill columns, which apply the discount to those households who are currently enrolled.

```{python}
#| label: burden-compute-lmi-current

below_current_lmi40, above_current_lmi40 = burden_shares(
    bills_current, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS,
)
below_hp_default_lmi40, above_hp_default_lmi40 = burden_shares(
    bills_hp, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS,
)
below_hp_seasonal_lmi40, above_hp_seasonal_lmi40 = burden_shares(
    bills_hprate_hp, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS,
)
below_hp_seasonal_lmi100, above_hp_seasonal_lmi100 = burden_shares(
    bills_hprate_hp, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df, bill_cols=LMI_100_BILL_COLS,
)

print(f"Current HVAC (40% enrolled):         {above_current_lmi40:.1f}% above 6%")
print(f"HP + default rates (40% enrolled):   {above_hp_default_lmi40:.1f}% above 6%")
print(f"HP + seasonal rates (40% enrolled):  {above_hp_seasonal_lmi40:.1f}% above 6%")
print(f"HP + seasonal rates (100% enrolled): {above_hp_seasonal_lmi100:.1f}% above 6%")

report_vars["pct_burdened_hp_seasonal_lmi100"] = above_hp_seasonal_lmi100 / 100
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-burden-bar-lmi-current
#| fig-cap: "Share of low-income gas-heated households above the 6% energy burden threshold"

plot_burden_bar(
    [below_current_lmi40, below_hp_default_lmi40, below_hp_seasonal_lmi40, below_hp_seasonal_lmi100],
    [above_current_lmi40, above_hp_default_lmi40, above_hp_seasonal_lmi40, above_hp_seasonal_lmi100],
)
```

#### By utility

```{python}
#| label: burden-compute-lmi-current-by-utility

burden_by_utility: dict[str, dict[str, tuple[float, float]]] = {}
for code in UTIL_ORDER_CODES:
    util_bldg_ids = low_income_gas_bldgs.filter(
        pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == code
    )[BLDG_ID].to_list()
    if len(util_bldg_ids) < 10:
        continue
    util_income = income_df.filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(util_bldg_ids))
    burden_by_utility[code] = {
        "current": burden_shares(bills_current, util_bldg_ids, util_income, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS),
        "hp_default": burden_shares(bills_hp, util_bldg_ids, util_income, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS),
        "hp_seasonal": burden_shares(bills_hprate_hp, util_bldg_ids, util_income, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS),
        "hp_seasonal_lmi100": burden_shares(bills_hprate_hp, util_bldg_ids, util_income, bill_cols=LMI_100_BILL_COLS),
    }

for code, data in burden_by_utility.items():
    print(f"{UTIL_NAMES[code]:20s}  Current: {data['current'][1]:5.1f}%  HP default: {data['hp_default'][1]:5.1f}%  HP seasonal: {data['hp_seasonal'][1]:5.1f}%  HP seasonal+100%LMI: {data['hp_seasonal_lmi100'][1]:5.1f}%")
```

```{python}
#| label: burden-bar-by-utility-helper

_BBU_BAR_HEIGHT = 0.55
_BBU_GROUP_GAP = 0.7
_BBU_INTRA_GAP = 0.15
_BBU_HEADER_SPACE = 0.55
_BBU_LABEL_SPACE = 0.35

_BURDEN_LABEL_ABOVE = "ENERGY BURDEN ABOVE 6%"
_BURDEN_LABEL_BELOW = "ENERGY BURDEN BELOW 6%"

_BURDEN_UTIL_SCENARIO_KEYS = ["current", "hp_default", "hp_seasonal", "hp_seasonal_lmi100"]
_BURDEN_UTIL_SCENARIO_LABELS = [
    f"Current HVAC\nDefault Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    f"Heat Pump\nDefault Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    f"Heat Pump\nHP Seasonal Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    "Heat Pump\nHP Seasonal Rates\n100% LMI Discounts",
]


def plot_burden_bar_by_utility(
    burden_data: dict[str, dict[str, tuple[float, float]]],
    utility_names: dict[str, str],
    scenario_keys: list[str] = _BURDEN_UTIL_SCENARIO_KEYS,
    scenario_labels: list[str] = _BURDEN_UTIL_SCENARIO_LABELS,
    title: str = "",
) -> None:
    """Diverging burden bars (above/below 6%) for each utility.

    Displays as inline SVG via IPython so Quarto's ``embed`` shortcode works.
    """
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

    ordered_codes = list(reversed(UTIL_ORDER_CODES))
    active_codes = [c for c in ordered_codes if c in burden_data]
    n_utils = len(active_codes)
    n_scenarios = len(scenario_keys)

    bar_positions: list[float] = []
    bar_labels: list[str] = []
    bar_above: list[float] = []
    bar_below: list[float] = []
    utility_header_ys: list[tuple[float, str]] = []
    label_rows: list[tuple[float, float, float]] = []

    y = 0.0
    for u_idx, code in enumerate(active_codes):
        utility_header_ys.append((y, utility_names[code]))
        y += _BBU_HEADER_SPACE

        first_below, first_above = burden_data[code][scenario_keys[0]]
        label_rows.append((y, first_above, first_below))
        y += _BBU_LABEL_SPACE

        for s_idx, key in enumerate(scenario_keys):
            below, above = burden_data[code][key]
            bar_positions.append(y)
            bar_labels.append(scenario_labels[s_idx])
            bar_above.append(above)
            bar_below.append(below)
            y += _BBU_BAR_HEIGHT + (_BBU_INTRA_GAP if s_idx < n_scenarios - 1 else 0)
        if u_idx < n_utils - 1:
            y += _BBU_GROUP_GAP

    y_max = y + 0.1
    fig_height = 1.5 + n_utils * 3.6

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12.5, fig_height))
    ax.set_xlim(-100, 100)
    ax.set_ylim(-0.1, y_max)
    ax.invert_yaxis()
    ax.set_xticks([])
    ax.set_yticks([])
    for spine in ax.spines.values():
        spine.set_visible(False)

    carrot = BURDEN_COLORS["Above 6%"]
    sky = BURDEN_COLORS["Below 6%"]

    for bp, above, below in zip(bar_positions, bar_above, bar_below):
        ax.barh(bp, above, left=0, height=_BBU_BAR_HEIGHT,
                color=carrot, edgecolor="none", align="edge")
        ax.barh(bp, -below, left=0, height=_BBU_BAR_HEIGHT,
                color=sky, edgecolor="none", align="edge")
        if above >= 3:
            ax.text(above / 2, bp + _BBU_BAR_HEIGHT / 2, f"{round(above)}%",
                    ha="center", va="center", color="white",
                    fontsize=10, fontweight="bold", fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans")
        if below >= 3:
            ax.text(-below / 2, bp + _BBU_BAR_HEIGHT / 2, f"{round(below)}%",
                    ha="center", va="center", color="white",
                    fontsize=10, fontweight="bold", fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans")

    for bp, label in zip(bar_positions, bar_labels):
        ax.text(101, bp + _BBU_BAR_HEIGHT / 2, label,
                ha="left", va="center", fontsize=8, fontweight="bold",
                fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans")

    for header_y, util_name in utility_header_ys:
        ax.text(-100, header_y, util_name,
                ha="left", va="top", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold",
                fontfamily="GT Planar", color="#333333")

    ax.axvline(x=0, color="#333333", linewidth=1.5)

    for label_y, first_above, first_below in label_rows:
        ax.text(first_above / 2, label_y, _BURDEN_LABEL_ABOVE,
                ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=8,
                fontweight="bold", color=carrot, fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans")
        ax.text(-first_below / 2, label_y, _BURDEN_LABEL_BELOW,
                ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=8,
                fontweight="bold", color=sky, fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans")

    fig.subplots_adjust(left=0.02, right=0.82, top=0.95, bottom=0.01)

    if title:
        ax_pos = ax.get_position()
        fig.text(ax_pos.x0, 0.97, title,
                 fontsize=12, fontweight="bold",
                 fontfamily="GT Planar", color="black", va="top")

    display_svg(fig)
```
pe

```{python}
#| label: fig-burden-bar-lmi-current-by-utility
#| fig-cap: "Share of low-income gas-heated households above the 6% energy burden threshold by utility, with current LMI discount enrollment (~40%)"

plot_burden_bar_by_utility(
    burden_by_utility, UTIL_NAMES,
    title="Energy burdens for LMI customers, by utility",
)
```

```{python}
#| label: burden-report-vars

report_vars["pct_burdened_current"] = above_current / 100
report_vars["pct_burdened_hp_default"] = above_hp_default / 100
report_vars["pct_burdened_hp_seasonal"] = above_hp_seasonal / 100
report_vars["pct_burdened_current_lmi40"] = above_current_lmi40 / 100
report_vars["pct_burdened_hp_default_lmi40"] = above_hp_default_lmi40 / 100
report_vars["pct_burdened_hp_seasonal_lmi40"] = above_hp_seasonal_lmi40 / 100
```

## Appendix: demand-flex elasticity calibration

```{python}
#| label: tbl-seasonal-elasticities

if INCLUDE_TOU:
    from great_tables import GT, loc, style, md

    _flex_diag_all = pl.read_parquet(
        "s3://data.sb/switchbox/demand_flex_calibration/ny/demand_flex_diagnostic.parquet"
    ) if "_flex_diag" not in dir() else _flex_diag

    _elast_tbl = (
        _flex_diag_all
        .filter(pl.col("elasticity") == pl.col("seasonal_recommended_elasticity"))
        .select(
            "utility", "season", "price_ratio",
            "seasonal_recommended_elasticity",
            "seasonal_arcturus_target_pct",
            "seasonal_recommended_savings",
            "seasonal_recommended_elasticity_with_tech",
            "seasonal_arcturus_target_pct_with_tech",
            "seasonal_recommended_savings_with_tech",
        )
        .with_columns(pl.col("utility").replace(UTIL_NAMES).alias("utility_name"))
        .sort(["utility_name", "season"])
        .to_pandas()
    )

    _gt_elast = (
        GT(_elast_tbl, rowname_col="season", groupname_col="utility_name")
        .cols_hide("utility")
        .cols_label(
            price_ratio=md("Price<br>ratio"),
            seasonal_recommended_elasticity=md("Elasticity<br>(no tech)"),
            seasonal_arcturus_target_pct=md("Arcturus target<br>(no tech, %)"),
            seasonal_recommended_savings=md("Savings/HP<br>(no tech, $)"),
            seasonal_recommended_elasticity_with_tech=md("Elasticity<br>(with tech)"),
            seasonal_arcturus_target_pct_with_tech=md("Arcturus target<br>(with tech, %)"),
            seasonal_recommended_savings_with_tech=md("Savings/HP<br>(with tech, $)"),
        )
        .fmt_number("price_ratio", decimals=2)
        .fmt_number(["seasonal_recommended_elasticity", "seasonal_recommended_elasticity_with_tech"], decimals=2)
        .fmt_number(["seasonal_arcturus_target_pct", "seasonal_arcturus_target_pct_with_tech"], decimals=1)
        .fmt_currency(["seasonal_recommended_savings", "seasonal_recommended_savings_with_tech"], decimals=0)
        .tab_header(
            title="Demand-flex elasticity calibration by utility and season",
            subtitle="Calibrated to Arcturus 2.0 meta-analysis; savings are annual rate arbitrage per HP building",
        )
        .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options())
    )
    display_gt(_gt_elast)
```

## Export report variables

```{python}
#| label: export-report-vars

import pickle

Path("../cache").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
Path("../cache/report_variables.pkl").write_bytes(pickle.dumps(report_vars))
```
